content:
[1] I love you, LORD — you are my strength.

[2] The LORD is my cliff, my fortress, my way out. My God is the rock I run to and disappear inside. He's my shield, the horn that saves me, my high tower.

[3] I call out to the One worthy of all praise — and just like that, I'm rescued from my enemies.

[4] Death's ropes coiled around me. Torrents of destruction crashed over my head.

[5] The grave's cords twisted tight. Death's traps sprang shut in front of me.

[6] Pinned down, I screamed for the LORD — cried out to my God. From his temple he heard my voice. My cry reached his ears.

[7] Then the earth bucked and shuddered. The mountains' foundations rocked — staggered — because he was furious.

[8] Smoke poured from his nostrils. Fire from his mouth devoured everything. Burning coals shot out of him.

[9] He ripped open the heavens and came down, storm-dark clouds beneath his feet.

[10] He mounted a cherub and flew. He soared on the wings of the wind.

[11] He wrapped darkness around himself like a cloak, his canopy the black weight of rain-soaked clouds.

[12] Out of the brightness in front of him, his clouds split open — hail and burning coals.

[13] The LORD thundered from heaven. The Most High loosed his voice — hail and burning coals.

[14] He shot his arrows and scattered them, hurled lightning and threw them into chaos.

[15] The valleys of the sea opened up. The foundations of the world stood naked — at the blast of your breath, LORD, at the snort of your fury.

[16] He reached down from on high. He took hold of me. He pulled me out of deep waters.

[17] He saved me from a powerful enemy, from those who hated me — they were too strong for me.

[18] They ambushed me on my worst day. But the LORD was the ground under my feet.

[19] He brought me out into wide open space. He rescued me because he delighted in me.

[20] The LORD rewarded me for doing right. He repaid me because my hands were clean.

[21] Because I kept his roads. I didn't turn away from my God toward evil.

[22] I kept his rulings in front of me. I didn't shove his decrees aside.

[23] I was blameless before him. I kept myself from the sin that was always reaching for me.

[24] So the LORD repaid me for doing right — for clean hands he could see.

[25] To the loyal you show yourself loyal. To the blameless you show yourself blameless.

[26] To the pure you are pure. But to the twisted you show yourself shrewd.

[27] You save the humbled. You stare down arrogant eyes until they drop.

[28] You — you light my lamp. The LORD my God turns my darkness to morning.

[29] With you I charge an army. With my God I scale a wall.

[30] God's way is flawless. The LORD's word has been tested by fire — it holds. He's a shield to everyone who runs to him for cover.

[31] Who is God besides the LORD? Who is a rock except our God?

[32] God is the one who straps strength around me and clears the road ahead.

[33] He makes my feet like a deer's — sure on the high places.

[34] He trains my hands for battle. My arms can bend a bow of bronze.

[35] You handed me the shield of your rescue. Your right hand held me up. Your gentleness made me great.

[36] You cleared a wide path for my stride. My ankles never gave out.

[37] I chased my enemies and caught them. I didn't turn back until they were finished.

[38] I crushed them — they couldn't get up. They fell beneath my feet.

[39] You strapped strength around me for the fight. You forced my attackers to their knees.

[40] You made my enemies turn and run. I cut down those who hated me.

[41] They screamed — no one came. Even to the LORD — he didn't answer.

[42] I ground them to dust the wind carries away. I poured them out like mud in the streets.

[43] You pulled me out of the people's quarrels. You set me at the head of nations. People I never knew now serve me.

[44] At the sound of my name they obey. Foreigners cringe before me.

[45] Foreigners lose their nerve. They come trembling out of their strongholds.

[46] The LORD lives! Blessed be my Rock! Lifted high be the God who saves me —

[47] the God who hands out justice on my behalf and brings nations under my feet,

[48] who tears me free from my enemies. You lifted me above my attackers. You snatched me from violent men.

[49] So I'll praise you, LORD, in front of the nations. I'll sing to your name.

[50] He hands his king staggering victories. He keeps his loyal love pouring out on the one he anointed — on David and his line, forever.

[1] The skies are shouting God's glory. The dome above is broadcasting what his hands have made. [2] Day after day pours out the message. Night after night passes on what it knows. [3] No speech. No words. Their voice goes unheard— [4] yet their signal travels the whole earth, their syllables reach the edge of the world.

There, in the heavens, he pitched a tent for the sun. [5] It emerges like a groom from his chamber, exultant as a champion sprinting his course. [6] It rises at one horizon and arcs across to the other— nothing escapes its heat.

[7] The instruction of the LORD is whole— it brings the soul back to life. The witness of the LORD holds firm— it makes the simple wise. [8] The orders of the LORD are straight— they set the heart singing. The command of the LORD is clear— it floods the eyes with light. [9] The fear of the LORD is clean— it lasts forever. The verdicts of the LORD are true— every one of them right down the line.

[10] More desirable than gold, than mountains of pure gold. Sweeter than honey, than honey straight from the comb.

[11] Your servant takes warning from them. In keeping them— deep reward.

[12] But who can spot his own missteps? Clear me of the faults I cannot see. [13] Hold your servant back from the arrogant sins— don't let them rule me. Then I will stand clean, innocent of the great rebellion.

[14] Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart find favor before you, LORD— my rock, my redeemer.

[1] May the LORD answer you when trouble closes in. May the name of Jacob's God lift you above the reach of it.

[2] May he send help from the sanctuary, strength from Zion to hold you up.

[3] May he remember every offering you've brought, accept every sacrifice you've laid down. *Selah.*

[4] May he give you what your heart is reaching for. May every plan you've made come through.

[5] When your victory comes, we'll erupt in shouting. We'll raise the banners in the name of our God. May the LORD answer everything you've asked.

[6] Now I know it — the LORD rescues his anointed. He answers from his holy heavens with the saving strength of his right hand.

[7] Some put their trust in chariots, some in horses. We trust the name of the LORD our God.

[8] They buckle. They fall. We rise and stand our ground.

[9] LORD, save the king. Answer us the day we call.

[1] The king celebrates your strength, LORD. Your victory — how wildly he rejoices in it.

[2] You handed him what his heart wanted. Whatever his lips asked, you did not withhold.

[3] You came out to meet him with rich blessing. You crowned his head with gold.

[4] He asked for life — you gave it. Length of days, forever and ever.

[5] Your victory wrapped him in glory. You draped him in splendor and majesty.

[6] You made him a fountain of blessing, forever. You flooded him with joy in your presence.

[7] The king trusts the LORD. Through the loyal love of the Most High, he will not be shaken.

[8] Your hand will find every enemy. Your right hand will hunt down everyone who hates you.

[9] The moment you appear, you will turn them to a blazing furnace. The LORD will swallow them in his fury. Fire will devour them.

[10] You will erase their offspring from the earth, their descendants from among the children of men.

[11] They schemed evil against you. They plotted — but they could not pull it off.

[12] You will put them to flight. You will draw your bow and aim straight at their faces.

[13] Rise up, LORD, in your strength. We will sing. We will make music for your power.

[1] My God, my God — why have you abandoned me? Why so far from saving me, so far from the words I'm howling?

[2] I cry out by day — you don't answer. By night — no rest, no silence in me.

[3] But you — you are the Holy One, enthroned on the praises of Israel.

[4] Our fathers trusted you. They trusted, and you pulled them free.

[5] They cried to you and broke into daylight. They leaned on you and were never put to shame.

[6] But I am a worm, not a man — mocked by humanity, despised by the crowd.

[7] Everyone who sees me sneers. They curl their lips, they shake their heads:

[8] "He trusted the LORD — let the LORD rescue him. Let him save him, since he loves him so much."

[9] But you — you drew me out of the womb. You set me trusting at my mother's breast.

[10] From birth I was thrown onto you. From my mother's body, you have been my God.

[11] Don't stay far from me — trouble is closing in, and there's no one to help.

[12] Bulls surround me — the massive bulls of Bashan ring me in.

[13] They open their jaws against me — lions tearing, roaring.

[14] I am poured out like water. Every bone in me has come unhinged. My heart has turned to wax, melting inside my chest.

[15] My strength is dried up — a piece of broken pottery. My tongue sticks to the roof of my mouth. You lay me down in the dust of death.

[16] Dogs are circling me. A pack of wicked men closes in. They have pierced my hands and my feet.

[17] I can count every bone in my body. They stare. They gloat over me.

[18] They split my clothes between them. They cast lots for what I wore.

[19] But you, LORD — don't stay far. You are my strength — hurry, help me.

[20] Rescue me from the sword. Save my one and only life from the grip of the dogs.

[21] Pull me from the lion's mouth. From the horns of the wild oxen — you have answered me.

[22] I will speak your name to my brothers. In the middle of the assembly, I will sing your praise.

[23] You who fear the LORD — praise him. All you children of Jacob — honor him. Stand in awe of him, every one of you, children of Israel.

[24] He has not despised the suffering of the afflicted. He has not turned his face away. When the broken one cried out, he heard.

[25] You are the source of my praise in the great assembly. Before all who fear him, I will fulfill my vows.

[26] The humble will eat and be satisfied. Those who seek the LORD will praise him. May your hearts live forever.

[27] Every corner of the earth will remember and turn to the LORD. Every family of every nation will bow before him.

[28] Because the kingdom belongs to the LORD. He rules the nations.

[29] Every prosperous soul on earth will feast and fall before him. Every mortal heading down to the dust will kneel — even the one who cannot keep himself alive.

[30] A generation will serve him. The next will be told about the Lord.

[31] They will come and declare his righteousness to a people yet unborn — that he has done it.

[1] The LORD is my shepherd. I lack nothing.

[2] He lays me down in green pastures. He leads me beside quiet water.

[3] He brings me back to life. He guides me down right paths because his name is on the line.

[4] Even when I walk through the valley swallowed in shadow, I will not fear what can harm me — because you are with me. Your rod, your staff — they tell me I am safe.

[5] You set a table for me in plain view of my enemies. You drench my head with oil. My cup spills over.

[6] Goodness and mercy will hunt me down every day of my life, and I will live in the house of the LORD as long as days run.

[1] The earth belongs to the LORD — every inch of it. The world and everyone walking on it.

[2] He laid its foundations on the seas, anchored it over the deep currents.

[3] Who gets to climb the LORD's mountain? Who gets to stand in his holy place?

[4] The one with clean hands and an uncluttered heart, who has not sold his soul to lies or sworn falsely to deceive.

[5] He walks away blessed by the LORD, vindicated by the God who saves him.

[6] These are the ones who seek him — the ones who come looking for the face of the God of Jacob.

[7] Lift your heads, you gates! Rise up, you ancient doors! The King of glory is coming through.

[8] Who is this King of glory? The LORD — strong, unstoppable. The LORD — a warrior who knows how to win.

[9] Lift your heads, you gates! Lift them, you ancient doors! The King of glory is coming through.

[10] Who is he, this King of glory? The LORD of armies — he is the King of glory.

[1] To you, LORD, I lift my soul.

[2] My God, I'm trusting you — don't let me crash and burn. Don't let my enemies stand over me grinning.

[3] No one who waits on you ends up disgraced. Disgrace belongs to the traitors, the ones who betray for nothing.

[4] Show me your roads, LORD. Teach me the paths you walk.

[5] Guide me into your truth. Train me. You are the God who rescues me — I wait for you all day long.

[6] Remember your mercy, LORD, your steady love. They've been there since before time began.

[7] The sins of my youth, my reckless years — forget them. Remember me instead through the lens of your love, because you are good, LORD.

[8] The LORD is good and straight. That's why he points sinners back to the road.

[9] He bends low to guide the humble. He teaches them his way.

[10] Every path the LORD walks is love and faithfulness — for those who keep covenant with him.

[11] For the sake of your name, LORD, pardon my guilt — it runs deep.

[12] Who is the one who fears the LORD? He'll be shown which road to choose.

[13] His life will settle into goodness. His descendants will inherit the land.

[14] The LORD takes the God-fearing into his confidence. He lets them in on the covenant.

[15] My eyes stay locked on the LORD. He's the one who frees my feet from the snare.

[16] Turn toward me. Show me grace. I'm alone. I'm crushed.

[17] My heart is in knots — pull me out of this distress.

[18] Look at my misery, my pain. Lift every sin off my shoulders.

[19] Look at my enemies — there are so many of them, and the hatred is violent.

[20] Guard my life. Rescue me. Don't let me be shamed — I've taken refuge in you.

[21] Let integrity and uprightness stand guard over me, because I wait for you.

[22] God — ransom Israel. Pull her out of every trouble.

[1] Vindicate me, LORD — I have walked with integrity. I trusted you and never wavered. Put me on trial.

[2] Test me. Probe me. Run the fire through my heart and mind.

[3] Your steadfast love is right in front of me. I walk by your truth.

[4] I don't sit with frauds. I don't run with hypocrites.

[5] I despise the gathering of the wicked. I refuse a seat among them.

[6] I wash my hands clean, LORD, and circle your altar,

[7] lifting a voice of thanksgiving, telling out every wonder you've done.

[8] I love the house where you live — the place where your glory rests.

[9] Don't sweep me away with sinners. Don't lump my life with men of blood,

[10] whose hands cradle their schemes, whose right hand is stuffed with bribes.

[11] But I — I walk with integrity. Redeem me. Show me mercy.

[12] My feet stand on level ground. In the great assembly I will bless the LORD.

[1] The LORD is my light and my rescue—who could make me afraid? The LORD is the stronghold of my life—who could make me tremble?

[2] When the wicked close in to devour me, when my enemies and foes mount their attack, they're the ones who stumble and fall.

[3] Let an army camp against me—my heart will not flinch. Let war break out against me—even then, I'll trust.

[4] One thing I've asked of the LORD, one thing I'm chasing: to live in the house of the LORD every day I'm alive, to drink in his beauty, to seek him in his temple.

[5] On the day disaster comes, he'll hide me in his shelter. He'll tuck me away in the secret place of his tent. He'll lift me high on a rock.

[6] And then—my head raised above the enemies surrounding me—I'll bring offerings in his tent with shouts of joy. I'll sing. I'll make music to the LORD.

[7] Hear me, LORD, when I cry out. Show me mercy. Answer me.

[8] My heart echoes back your words: "Seek my face." Your face, LORD—that's what I'm seeking.

[9] Don't turn your face from me. Don't push your servant away in anger—you've been my help. Don't abandon me, don't walk away, God of my rescue.

[10] Even if my father and mother walked out on me, the LORD would gather me up.

[11] Teach me your way, LORD. Lead me on a level path, because my enemies are watching.

[12] Don't hand me over to what they want—false witnesses have risen against me, breathing out violence.

[13] I'm still convinced of this: I will see the goodness of the LORD in the land of the living.

[14] Wait for the LORD. Be strong. Let your heart take courage. Wait for the LORD.

[1] You — LORD — you are who I call to. My Rock, do not go deaf on me. If you stay silent, I'm finished — just one more body sliding into the pit.

[2] Hear me begging. Hear the cry tearing out of me as I lift my hands toward your holy sanctuary, toward the inner place where you dwell.

[3] Don't drag me off with the wicked, with the ones who traffic in evil — the ones who smile and speak peace to their neighbors while plotting ruin in their hearts.

[4] Give them what their actions deserve. Pay them back for the harm their hands have built. Hand them the wages they've earned.

[5] Because they refuse to see what the LORD does, refuse to notice the work of his hands — so he will tear them down and never rebuild them.

[6] Blessed be the LORD. He heard me. He heard the sound of my pleading.

[7] The LORD is my strength, my shield. My heart trusted him and help came running. My body is alive with it — I sing back to him with everything I have.

[8] The LORD is strength for his people, a fortress of rescue for the one he anointed.

[9] Save your people. Bless what belongs to you. Shepherd them. Carry them — forever.

[1] Give it to the LORD, you sons of heaven— give him the glory, give him the strength. [2] Give the LORD the weight his name deserves. Bow before him in the splendor of holiness.

[3] The voice of the LORD breaks over the waters. The God of glory thunders. The LORD rides the deep.

[4] The voice of the LORD — sheer power. The voice of the LORD — sheer majesty.

[5] The voice of the LORD splinters cedars. The LORD shatters the cedars of Lebanon.

[6] He makes Lebanon leap like a calf, Sirion like a young wild ox.

[7] The voice of the LORD strikes — flames of fire splitting the sky.

[8] The voice of the LORD shakes the wilderness. The LORD shakes the wilderness of Kadesh.

[9] The voice of the LORD twists the oaks and strips the forests bare. And in his temple — every soul cries, *Glory!*

[10] The LORD sat enthroned over the flood. The LORD sits enthroned — king, forever.

[11] The LORD gives strength to his people. The LORD blesses his people with peace.

[1] I lift you high, LORD — you pulled me up from the pit, you didn't let my enemies dance over my grave.

[2] LORD my God, I cried out to you for help, and you healed me.

[3] LORD, you hauled my soul up from the realm of the dead. You kept me breathing when I was already sliding down into the grave.

[4] Sing to the LORD, all of you who belong to him. Give thanks at the memory of his holiness.

[5] His anger lasts a moment; his favor lasts a lifetime. Tears check in for the night — joy arrives at dawn.

[6] When everything was easy, I told myself: "Nothing can shake me."

[7] LORD, in your favor you made me a mountain — solid, unmovable. Then you hid your face, and I came apart.

[8] So I called out to you, LORD. I begged the Lord for mercy:

[9] "What's the profit if you spill my blood, if I drop into the pit? Can dust praise you? Can it announce your faithfulness?

[10] Hear me, LORD. Show me mercy. LORD, be the one who carries me."

[11] And you did it — you spun my grief into dancing. You stripped off my mourning clothes and dressed me in joy,

[12] so my soul can sing to you and never shut up. LORD my God, I will thank you forever.

[1] In you, Lord, I take cover — never let me end up disgraced. Pull me out of this by your righteousness. [2] Lean down. Listen. Get to me fast. Be the rock I run to, the fortress that holds. [3] You are my cliff and my stronghold — for the sake of your own name, lead me, guide me out. [4] Spring me from the trap they've hidden for me. You are my shelter. [5] Into your hands I place my life — you've already bought me back, Lord, God of truth.

[6] I have no use for people who chase empty gods. I trust the Lord. [7] I'll dance in your steady love, because you saw what was crushing me — you knew the agony inside me — [8] and you didn't hand me over to the enemy. You set my feet down in wide-open country.

[9] Be gracious to me, Lord — I'm hemmed in. My eyes burn through with grief, and so does my body, so does my soul. [10] My life is being spent on sorrow, my years on groaning. My strength buckles under what I've done wrong. My bones are giving way. [11] To every enemy I'm a joke — and worse to my neighbors, a horror to anyone who knows me. People on the street see me coming and bolt. [12] I've slipped out of memory like a corpse. I'm a broken pot. [13] I hear them whispering — terror on every side — plotting together, scheming how to take my life.

[14] But I — I trust you, Lord. I said it: you are my God. [15] My times are in your hand. Rescue me from the grip of my enemies, from those hunting me down. [16] Let your face light up over your servant. Save me by your steady love. [17] Don't let me be shamed, Lord — I called out to you. Let the wicked be shamed instead, silenced in the grave. [18] Shut the mouths that lie, the arrogant tongues spitting contempt at the righteous.

[19] How vast is the goodness you've stored up for those who fear you, poured out for those who shelter in you — and the whole world watching. [20] You hide them in the cover of your presence, far from human plots; you tuck them away in your shelter, safe from the war of tongues.

[21] Blessed be the Lord — his steady love astonished me, like a city under siege that suddenly holds. [22] I had panicked. I'd said, "I'm cut off from your sight." But you heard me crying for help when I called.

[23] Love the Lord, all you who belong to him. He keeps the faithful — and he pays back, in full, the proud. [24] Be strong. Let your heart take courage, all of you waiting on the Lord.

[1] Lucky is the one whose rebellion is lifted off, whose failure is buried out of sight. [2] Lucky is the one the LORD refuses to charge with guilt — the one whose spirit holds no deceit.

[3] When I kept silent, my bones wasted away — I groaned through the day, every day. [4] Day and night your hand pressed down on me; my strength dried up like ground in summer heat. *Selah*

[5] Then I owned it. I stopped hiding what I'd done. I said, "I'll lay my rebellion before the LORD" — and you lifted the weight of my guilt clean off. *Selah*

[6] So let every faithful one pray to you while you can still be found. When the floodwaters rise, they will not reach them. [7] You are my hiding place. You guard me from the squeeze. You ring me with songs of rescue. *Selah*

[8] I'll teach you. I'll show you the road to walk. I'll keep my eye on you and guide you. [9] Don't be like a horse or a mule — mindless, needing bit and bridle just to come near you.

[10] The wicked have pain stacked on pain. But the one who trusts the LORD — steadfast love surrounds them. [11] Celebrate in the LORD, you who are right with him. Sing out loud, all of you whose hearts run straight.

[1] Sing out, you who walk with him—praise looks good on the upright.
[2] Strike the harp in thanks. Make music on the ten-stringed lyre.
[3] Write him a new song. Play with skill, play with fire.

[4] Because his word rings true and everything he does is solid.
[5] He's in love with justice and fairness; the earth is soaked through with his loyal love.

[6] One word from him and the heavens existed. One breath, and the whole star-strewn host.
[7] He scooped the seas into a jar, locked the deeps in his vaults.

[8] Let the whole earth hold its breath before him. Let everyone alive stand in awe.
[9] Because he spoke—and it was. He commanded—and it stood there.

[10] He shreds the plans of nations. He cancels what the peoples scheme.
[11] But his own plan stands forever. What he intends moves generation to generation.

[12] Lucky is the nation whose God is the LORD—the people he chose as his own inheritance.

[13] From the heavens he looks down. He sees every human face.
[14] From his throne-room he watches everyone who lives on earth—
[15] he who shaped every heart, he who reads everything they do.

[16] No king is saved by the size of his army. No warrior survives by sheer muscle.
[17] Counting on a horse for rescue is a fool's bet—all that power can't save you.

[18] But look—the LORD's eye is on those who revere him, on those who wait for his loyal love
[19] to pull them back from death, to keep them alive when the famine bites down.

[20] So we wait for the LORD. He's our help. He's our shield.
[21] Our hearts come alive in him because we've trusted his holy name.
[22] Let your loyal love rest on us, LORD—the way we've rested our hope in you.

[1] I will bless the LORD every moment of my life. His praise will never leave my mouth.

[2] My soul makes its boast in him — let the broken hear it and come alive.

[3] Make him great with me. Together, let us lift his name high.

[4] I went looking for the LORD, and he answered. He pulled me out from under every fear I carried.

[5] Look to him and your face catches light — shame will never darken it again.

[6] This nobody cried out, and the LORD heard. He saved me from every tight corner.

[7] The angel of the LORD pitches camp around those who fear him, and he gets them out alive.

[8] Taste it for yourself — see how good the LORD is. Blessed is the one who runs to him for shelter.

[9] Fear the LORD, you who belong to him — those who fear him want for nothing.

[10] Young lions go hungry and prowl in vain, but those who seek the LORD lack no good thing.

[11] Come here, children. Listen to me. I will teach you what it means to fear the LORD.

[12] Who is it that loves life? Who wants days worth living, days full of good?

[13] Then keep your tongue from evil, your lips from telling lies.

[14] Turn away from wrong. Do good. Hunt for peace and chase it down.

[15] The LORD's eyes are on the righteous, his ears tuned to every cry they make.

[16] But his face is set against those who do evil — to wipe their memory off the earth.

[17] The righteous cry out, the LORD hears, and he hauls them out of every disaster.

[18] The LORD stays close to the brokenhearted. He rescues those whose spirit lies in pieces.

[19] The righteous endure trouble after trouble — and the LORD delivers them out of every one.

[20] He guards their bones — not one of them will be broken.

[21] Evil kills the wicked. Those who hate the righteous will pay for it.

[22] The LORD redeems the life of his servants. Take refuge in him, and condemnation cannot touch you.

[1] Fight my fighters, LORD. Make war on those who make war on me.

[2] Strap on shield and armor. Rise up. Come help me.

[3] Pull out spear and javelin to block my pursuers. Tell my soul: I am your rescue.

[4] Let those hunting my life be shamed and humiliated. Let those plotting my ruin stumble back in disgrace.

[5] Make them chaff in the wind, with the angel of the LORD driving them off.

[6] Make their road dark and slick, with the angel of the LORD chasing them down.

[7] They hid their net for me — no reason. They dug a pit for my life — no reason.

[8] So let ruin ambush them when they least expect it. Let the net they hid catch their own feet. Let them fall into the pit they dug.

[9] Then my soul will dance in the LORD, will celebrate his rescue.

[10] Every bone in me will say: LORD, who is like you? You snatch the weak from those too strong for them, the poor and needy from those who would strip them bare.

[11] Witnesses show up swearing lies, grilling me about things I know nothing about.

[12] They pay back my good with evil. My soul sits bereaved.

[13] But when they were sick, I wore sackcloth. I starved my body with fasting. My prayers kept returning to my own chest, unanswered.

[14] I walked around grieving for them like a friend, like a brother. I bent low in mourning like someone weeping for his mother.

[15] But when I stumbled, they threw a party. They gathered against me — strangers I never knew — ripping me apart and never stopping.

[16] Like godless mockers at a feast, they ground their teeth at me.

[17] Lord, how long will you watch this? Pull my soul back from their wrecking, my one life from these lions.

[18] I will thank you in the great assembly. Among a roaring crowd I will praise you.

[19] Do not let my treacherous enemies gloat over me. Do not let those who hate me without cause exchange winks behind my back.

[20] They do not speak peace. They cook up lies against quiet people in the land.

[21] They open their mouths wide at me, sneering: Ha! Ha! We saw it with our own eyes!

[22] You saw it too, LORD. Do not stay silent. Lord, do not stay far from me.

[23] Wake up. Rise to my defense. My God, my Lord — take up my case.

[24] Vindicate me, LORD my God, by your justice. Do not let them gloat over me.

[25] Do not let them say in their hearts: Yes! Just what we wanted. Do not let them say: We swallowed him whole.

[26] Let those who celebrate my ruin be shamed and humiliated together. Let those who tower over me be wrapped in shame and disgrace.

[27] But those who want to see me cleared — let them shout for joy, let them celebrate. Let them say always: The LORD is great — he delights in the welfare of his servant.

[28] Then my tongue will speak your justice, will praise you all day long.

[1] Deep inside the wicked, an oracle speaks — sin whispers to their heart, and they listen. There is no fear of God before their eyes.

[2] They flatter themselves so thoroughly they can't even see their own guilt, can't bring themselves to hate it.

[3] Every word from their mouth is poison and deceit. They've abandoned wisdom. They've abandoned doing good.

[4] They lie awake at night plotting trouble. They plant their feet on a road that leads nowhere good — and they refuse to call evil what it is.

[5] But your love, Lord — it stretches to the heavens. Your faithfulness reaches the clouds.

[6] Your justice towers like the great mountains. Your judgments run deeper than the sea. You rescue everything that breathes — humans, animals, all of it.

[7] How precious is your love, O God! Every creature finds shelter in the shadow of your wings.

[8] They feast on the abundance of your house. You let them drink from the river of your delights.

[9] With you is the fountain of life. In your light we see light.

[10] Keep pouring your love on those who know you, your righteousness on the honest of heart.

[11] Don't let the foot of the arrogant trample me. Don't let the hand of the wicked drive me from my place.

[12] Look — there they fall, the evildoers. Flung down. Unable to rise.

[1] Don't let the success of the wicked eat at you. Don't burn with envy when crooks prosper.
[2] They're grass — green this morning, brown by afternoon, gone by sundown.

[3] Trust God. Do good. Settle into the land and feed on its faithfulness.
[4] Delight yourself in him, and he'll plant the deepest desires of your heart and bring them to harvest.

[5] Hand him your road. Trust him. He'll act.
[6] He'll bring your integrity blazing into the open like sunrise, your vindication like the high noon sun.

[7] Be still before God. Wait for him. Don't agitate yourself over the one whose schemes pay off, the one whose evil plans succeed.
[8] Stop the simmering rage. Walk away from fury. Don't let it pull you in — it only leads to more evil.

[9] The wicked get cut down. Those who wait on God inherit the earth.
[10] Give it a little while — the wicked won't be there. You'll look for them in their old place and find nothing.
[11] But the humble inherit the land. They settle into a peace so deep it overflows.

[12] The wicked plot against the righteous and grind their teeth in hatred,
[13] but the Lord laughs at them. He sees their day coming.

[14] They've drawn their swords, strung their bows, aiming at the poor and the helpless, hunting the honest down.
[15] Their swords will pierce their own hearts. Their bows will splinter in their hands.

[16] Better the little a righteous person has than the bloated wealth of the wicked.
[17] The arms of the wicked will be broken. The Lord himself props up the righteous.

[18] God knows every day of the blameless. Their inheritance is forever.
[19] When disaster hits, they won't be ruined. When famine comes, they'll eat their fill.

[20] But the wicked perish. The Lord's enemies are like the glory of pastures — beautiful, brief, vanishing into smoke.

[21] The wicked borrow and never repay. The righteous give freely, openhanded.
[22] Those God blesses inherit the land. Those he curses are cut off.

[23] A good person's steps are firmed up by the Lord — he loves the road they're walking.
[24] Even when they stumble, they don't fall flat — the Lord catches their hand.

[25] I was young once. Now I'm old. And I have never seen the righteous abandoned, never seen their children begging bread.
[26] All day long they're generous, lending freely — and their children become a blessing.

[27] Turn from evil. Do good. You'll live in the land forever.
[28] The Lord loves justice. He never abandons his faithful ones — they're guarded for all time. But the line of the wicked gets cut.

[29] The righteous inherit the land and live on it forever.
[30] Wisdom flows from the righteous mouth. Justice shapes every word.
[31] The law of their God is written on their heart. Their feet don't slide.

[32] The wicked stalk the righteous, hunting for a chance to kill.
[33] The Lord won't leave them in those hands, won't let them be condemned when dragged to trial.

[34] Wait for the Lord. Stay on his road. He'll lift you up to inherit the land. You'll witness the wicked being cut down.

[35] I saw a wicked man — ruthless, towering, spreading himself like a native cedar.
[36] I passed by again and he was gone. I searched. He couldn't be found.

[37] Watch the man of integrity. Study the upright. There's a future for the person of peace.
[38] But sinners are wiped out together. The future of the wicked is annihilation.

[39] The rescue of the righteous comes from the Lord — he is their fortress in the day everything caves in.
[40] The Lord stands by them, pulls them clear. He delivers them from the wicked and saves them, because they took shelter in him.

[1] LORD — don't tear into me in your fury. Don't discipline me in the heat of your wrath.

[2] Your arrows have buried themselves in me. Your hand has come down hard.

[3] There's no health left in my body — your anger has seen to that. No peace in my bones — my sin has seen to that.

[4] My guilt has risen over my head like floodwater, a weight too heavy to carry.

[5] My wounds fester. They stink. And it's my own foolishness that opened them.

[6] I'm bent double, brought low, dragging myself through the day in mourning clothes.

[7] My back is on fire. There's nothing sound left in me.

[8] I'm spent. Crushed. My heart roars with a groan that won't quit.

[9] Lord, every longing I have is laid out before you. My sighing isn't hidden from you.

[10] My heart pounds wildly. My strength has drained away. Even the light in my eyes has gone dark.

[11] Friends and companions hang back from my wounds. My own family stands at a distance.

[12] Those hunting my life are setting their snares. Those wanting to ruin me speak destruction — plotting deceit all day long.

[13] But I'm like a deaf man — I hear nothing. Like a mute — I open no mouth.

[14] I've become a man who cannot hear, whose lips frame no reply.

[15] Because it's you, LORD, I'm waiting for. You will answer, my Lord, my God.

[16] I said: don't let them gloat over me — those who strut when my foot slips.

[17] I'm on the verge of falling. The pain never leaves me.

[18] So I confess my guilt. I'm sick over my sin.

[19] Meanwhile my enemies thrive — full of life, multiplying. Those who hate me for no reason keep stacking up.

[20] They pay me back evil for good. They oppose me for chasing what's right.

[21] Don't abandon me, LORD. My God — don't stay far off.

[22] Come quickly to help me, Lord — my salvation.

[1] I told myself: I'll watch my steps. I won't sin with my tongue. I'll keep a muzzle on my mouth while the wicked are standing in front of me.

[2] So I went silent — total silence, not a word, not even about what was good. And my pain only sharpened.

[3] My heart burned inside me. The longer I brooded, the hotter the fire — until finally I had to speak:

[4] Lord, show me how this ends. Show me the count of my days. Let me see how fragile I really am.

[5] Look — you've made my life a few handbreadths long. My whole existence is nothing to you. Every human being, even standing tall, is only a breath. *Selah*

[6] We drift through life like shadows. We churn ourselves up over nothing. We pile up wealth and don't even know who'll end up with it.

[7] So now — what am I waiting for, Lord? My hope is in you. Only you.

[8] Pull me out of every rebellion I've fallen into. Don't let the fool make a mockery of me.

[9] I've gone quiet. I won't open my mouth — because you are the one who did this.

[10] Lift this blow off me. The strike of your hand is destroying me.

[11] When you discipline a person for their guilt, you consume what they love like a moth eating cloth. Every human is only a breath. *Selah*

[12] Hear my prayer, Lord. Listen to my cry. Don't be deaf to my tears. I'm a stranger here, passing through, like every ancestor before me.

[13] Look away from me. Let me catch my breath before I'm gone — before I'm nothing at all.

[1] I waited. I waited for the LORD, and he bent down to me and heard my cry.

[2] He hauled me up out of the roaring pit, out of the sucking mud, and set my feet on rock — gave me ground that would hold.

[3] He put a new song in my mouth, a song of praise to our God. Many will see it, and they will be struck with awe, and they will trust the LORD.

[4] Blessed is the one who anchors their trust in the LORD — who doesn't turn toward the arrogant, doesn't drift after lies.

[5] LORD my God, the wonders you've done, the plans you've laid for us — no one can line them up beside you. If I tried to count them, to tell them all, they would outrun me. There are too many.

[6] You didn't want sacrifice and offering — you opened my ears instead. Burnt offerings and sin offerings — you never asked for them.

[7] So I said: Here I come. The scroll is written about me.

[8] Doing what you want, my God — that's my delight. Your instruction lives inside me.

[9] I've announced your righteousness in the great assembly. I haven't shut my lips — LORD, you know it.

[10] I haven't buried your righteousness in my heart. I've spoken your faithfulness, your saving power. I haven't hidden your steady love and your truth from the great assembly.

[11] So you, LORD — don't withhold your mercy from me. Let your steady love and your truth guard me always.

[12] Troubles have closed in around me — more than I can count. My sins have caught me, and I can't see my way out. They outnumber the hairs on my head, and my heart has failed me.

[13] LORD — be willing. Rescue me. LORD — hurry. Help me.

[14] Let those hunting my life be humiliated and disgraced together. Let those who delight in my ruin be driven back in shame.

[15] Let them be appalled at their own disgrace — those who jeer over me, "Aha! Aha!"

[16] But let everyone who seeks you celebrate and rejoice in you. Let those who love your salvation say it without ceasing: The LORD is great.

[17] As for me — I'm poor and needy, but the Lord is thinking of me. You are my help, my rescuer. My God — don't wait.

[1] Blessed is the one who pays attention to the weak. When trouble comes, the LORD pulls him out.

[2] The LORD guards his life, keeps him alive, calls him blessed in the land — and refuses to hand him over to the appetite of his enemies.

[3] When sickness flattens him to his bed, the LORD is there, nursing him back. The LORD remakes the whole sickbed into something else entirely.

[4] So I said: LORD, show me grace. Heal me. I've sinned against you.

[5] My enemies whisper venom about me: "When will he finally die? When does his name disappear?"

[6] One comes to visit and his mouth is empty — his heart is busy stockpiling slander. He walks out the door and starts talking.

[7] Together they hiss against me, every one of them, plotting the worst:

[8] "Some deadly thing has fastened on him. He's down. He's never getting up."

[9] Even the friend I trusted — the one who ate at my table — has lifted his heel against me.

[10] But you, LORD — show me grace. Lift me up. Let me give them what they have coming.

[11] This is how I know you delight in me: my enemy doesn't get the last word over me.

[12] You hold me steady because I'm honest, and you set me in your presence — forever.

[13] Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from forever to forever. Amen and amen.

[1] Like a deer gasping for streams of water, my soul gasps for you, God.

[2] My soul thirsts for God — for the living God. When can I come and stand before his face?

[3] Tears have been my food day and night, while they ask me, relentless: "Where is this God of yours?"

[4] I remember — and it breaks me open — how I used to move with the crowd, leading them in procession to the house of God, voices ringing with joy and thanksgiving, a festival roar.

[5] Why are you collapsing, my soul? Why this churning inside me? Wait for God. I will praise him again — my rescue, my God.

[6] My soul caves in on itself. So I remember you from the land of Jordan, from the peaks of Hermon, from the small hill called Mizar.

[7] Deep calls to deep in the thunder of your waterfalls. Every wave, every breaker you send — they all crash over me.

[8] By day the Lord commands his steadfast love, and by night his song stays with me — a prayer to the God of my life.

[9] I say to God, my rock: "Why have you forgotten me? Why must I walk in mourning while the enemy crushes me?"

[10] Their taunts cut to the bone as they hammer me all day: "Where is this God of yours?"

[11] Why are you collapsing, my soul? Why this churning inside me? Wait for God. I will praise him again — my rescue, my God.

[1] Judge me, God. Take up my case against a nation with no loyalty. From the deceitful, the unjust man — rescue me.

[2] You are the God where I take shelter. Why have you cast me off? Why do I walk in mourning, crushed beneath my enemy?

[3] Send out your light. Send out your truth. Let them lead me — let them bring me to your holy mountain, to the place where you dwell.

[4] Then I will come to the altar of God, to God my joy, my exultation. With the harp I will praise you — God, my God.

[5] Why are you sinking, my soul? Why this storm inside me? Wait for God. I will praise him again — my rescue, my God.

[1] God, we heard it with our own ears — our fathers told us the story, what you accomplished in their day, back in the ancient times.

[2] With your own hand you tore nations out by the roots and planted our fathers in their place. You crushed peoples and made our fathers spread.

[3] They didn't take the land by their swords. Their arms won them nothing. It was your right hand, your arm, the light of your face — because you loved them.

[4] You are my King, O God. Command victory for Jacob.

[5] Through you we drive back our enemies. In your name we trample those who rise against us.

[6] I don't trust my bow. My sword won't save me.

[7] You — you are the one who saves us from our enemies. You are the one who shames those who hate us.

[8] In God we boast all day long. We will praise your name forever.

[9] But now — you've rejected us. You've humiliated us. You no longer march out with our armies.

[10] You make us retreat from the enemy. Those who hate us help themselves to our spoil.

[11] You hand us over like sheep to be eaten. You scatter us among the nations.

[12] You sell your people for nothing — you didn't even haggle for the price.

[13] You make us the laughingstock of our neighbors, the mockery and scorn of everyone around us.

[14] You make us a proverb among the nations — heads shaking at the sight of us.

[15] My disgrace stares me in the face all day. Shame covers me —

[16] from the voice of the taunter, the slanderer, the face of the enemy, the avenger.

[17] All this has come on us — and we have not forgotten you. We have not betrayed your covenant.

[18] Our hearts haven't turned back. Our feet haven't strayed from your path.

[19] Yet you crushed us in the place where jackals prowl. You covered us with the shadow of death.

[20] If we had forgotten the name of our God, if we had reached out our hands to some foreign god —

[21] wouldn't God have uncovered it? He knows the secrets of the heart.

[22] No — for your sake we are being killed all day long. We are counted as sheep marked for slaughter.

[23] Wake up! Why are you sleeping, Lord? Rouse yourself! Don't reject us forever.

[24] Why are you hiding your face? Why do you forget our affliction, our oppression?

[25] We are crushed into the dust. Our bodies cling to the ground.

[26] Rise up — help us! Redeem us, because of your steadfast love.

[1] My heart is stirring with a beautiful theme; I'm reciting my verses for the king. My tongue is the pen of a master scribe.

[2] You are the finest of men. Grace pours from your lips. God has blessed you forever.

[3] Strap your sword to your thigh, warrior — clothe yourself in splendor and majesty.

[4] Ride out victorious for truth, for humility, for justice. Let your right hand teach you terrifying things.

[5] Your arrows are sharp — they pierce the heart of the king's enemies. Nations collapse beneath you.

[6] Your throne, O God, stands forever and ever. A scepter of justice is the scepter of your kingdom.

[7] You loved righteousness and hated wickedness — so God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of joy above every companion you have.

[8] Your robes breathe myrrh, aloes, cassia. From ivory palaces, the music of strings makes you glad.

[9] Daughters of kings are among your honored ones. At your right hand stands the queen in gold from Ophir.

[10] Listen, daughter — pay attention, lean in. Forget your people. Forget your father's house.

[11] The king is captivated by your beauty. He is your lord now — bow to him.

[12] The daughter of Tyre will come with a gift. The wealthy of the people will seek your favor.

[13] The king's daughter is glorious within her chamber — her gown woven with threads of gold.

[14] In embroidered robes she is led to the king. Her companions, the virgins behind her, are brought to you.

[15] With joy and gladness they enter the palace of the king.

[16] In place of your fathers will be your sons — you will make them princes over all the earth.

[17] I will make your name remembered through every generation. So the peoples will praise you forever and ever.

[1] God is our shelter and our strength, the help that shows up when everything falls apart.

[2] So we will not flinch — not when the earth dissolves beneath us, not when the mountains slide into the heart of the sea,

[3] not when the waters roar and churn and the peaks tremble at their fury. *Selah*

[4] There is a river — its streams make glad the city of God, the sacred dwelling of the Most High.

[5] God is inside her. She will not fall. God will come to her aid at the break of dawn.

[6] Nations rage. Kingdoms collapse. He raises his voice — and the earth melts.

[7] The LORD of armies is with us. The God of Jacob is our stronghold. *Selah*

[8] Come — look at what the LORD has done, the desolations he has carved into the earth.

[9] He silences wars from one end of the world to the other. He snaps the bow, shatters the spear, and sets the war-chariots ablaze.

[10] "Stop. Be still. Know that I am God. I will be exalted among the nations. I will be exalted in the earth."

[11] The LORD of armies is with us. The God of Jacob is our stronghold. *Selah*

[1] Clap your hands, all you peoples! Shout to God with the roar of triumph!

[2] Because the LORD Most High is terrifying—the great King ruling over all the earth.

[3] He drove nations down beneath us, pressed peoples under our feet.

[4] He picked our inheritance for us—the pride of Jacob, the one he loved.

[5] God has ascended with a shout, the LORD with the blast of the trumpet.

[6] Sing to God, sing! Sing to our King, sing!

[7] Because God is King of all the earth—sing a song that knows what it's doing.

[8] God reigns over the nations. God is seated on his holy throne.

[9] The princes of the peoples gather as the people of Abraham's God—because the shields of the earth belong to God. He is lifted high above them all.

[1] Great is the LORD, worthy of every shout of praise, in the city of our God, on his holy mountain.

[2] Beautiful in its rise, the joy of the whole earth — Mount Zion, the high northern slopes, the city of the great King.

[3] God himself walks her ramparts. He is the fortress within the fortress.

[4] Look — the kings allied. They marched together.

[5] They saw her. They froze. Panic took them. They scattered.

[6] Trembling seized them right there — pain like a woman giving birth.

[7] You shattered them like the east wind shatters the ships of Tarshish.

[8] What we had only heard, now we have seen — in the city of the LORD of armies, the city of our God. God will hold her steady. Forever.

[9] Inside your temple, O God, we turn over your unfailing love in our minds.

[10] Your name reaches the ends of the earth, and so does your praise. Your right hand is full of justice.

[11] Let Mount Zion celebrate. Let the daughters of Judah dance — because of your verdicts.

[12] Walk around Zion. Circle her. Count her towers.

[13] Study her walls. Inspect her citadels. So you can tell the next generation:

[14] This God — this is our God, forever and ever. He himself will lead us, even past death.

[1] Listen up, every nation. Pay attention, everyone who lives on this earth—

[2] high-born and low-born, rich and poor, all of you together.

[3] What comes out of my mouth is wisdom; what my heart has worked over is understanding.

[4] I'll tune my ear to a proverb, unlock the riddle with my harp.

[5] Why should I be afraid when bad times come, when the schemers at my heels close in—

[6] the ones who lean on their bank accounts, who brag about how much they've piled up?

[7] No one can buy back another person's life. No one can hand God the ransom price—

[8] the cost of a soul is too steep. Stop trying. You'll never have enough,

[9] never enough to live forever and skip the grave.

[10] Look around. The wise die. So do fools and idiots. They all leave their fortunes to strangers.

[11] Their graves become their permanent address, their houses for all generations—even though they once stamped their names across the land.

[12] Wealth doesn't make a person last. They die just like the animals.

[13] This is the path of the self-assured, and of everyone who applauds their talk. *Selah*

[14] They're herded like sheep into the realm of the dead. Death is their shepherd. Morning comes and the upright walk over them; their bodies rot far from the mansions they built.

[15] But God will buy my soul back from the grave's grip. He will take me. *Selah*

[16] Don't let it rattle you when someone gets rich, when their estate grows enormous.

[17] They take nothing with them when they die. The glory doesn't follow them down.

[18] They congratulated themselves while alive—and people praise you when you do well for yourself—

[19] but they'll join the line of their ancestors and never see light again.

[20] A person with all the wealth in the world but no understanding is just an animal that perishes.

[1] The Mighty One, God, the LORD, has spoken. He summons the earth from where the sun rises to where it sets. [2] From Zion — beauty made perfect — God blazes forth. [3] Our God comes, and he will not keep silent. Fire devours ahead of him; a tempest rages around him. [4] He calls the heavens above and the earth below to witness the trial of his people: [5] "Gather my faithful ones to me — those who sealed a covenant with me by sacrifice." [6] The heavens proclaim his justice, for God himself is the Judge. *Selah*

[7] "Listen, my people, and I will speak. Israel, I am bringing charges against you. I am God — your God. [8] My quarrel isn't about your sacrifices. Your burnt offerings stand before me always. [9] I don't need a bull from your barn or goats from your pens. [10] Every animal in the forest already belongs to me, and the cattle on a thousand hills. [11] I know every bird in the mountains; every creature in the field is mine. [12] If I were hungry, I wouldn't tell you — the world is mine, and everything in it. [13] Do I eat the meat of bulls? Do I drink the blood of goats? [14] Offer God your thanksgiving. Make good on your vows to the Most High. [15] Call on me when trouble comes — I will rescue you, and you will give me glory."

[16] But to the wicked God says: "What right do you have to recite my statutes or take my covenant on your lips? [17] You hate correction. You toss my words behind you. [18] You see a thief and run with him. You throw in your lot with adulterers. [19] Your mouth spills evil. Your tongue weaves deceit. [20] You sit there slandering your own brother — smearing your mother's son. [21] You did all this while I held my peace, and you assumed I was just like you. But now I'm calling you out. I'm laying it bare before your eyes.

[22] "Take this in, you who forget God — or I will tear you apart, and no one will save you. [23] Whoever offers thanksgiving honors me. And to the one who orders his way rightly, I will show the salvation of God."

[1] God — have mercy on me. Out of your steady love. Out of the depth of your tenderness, wipe away what I've done. [2] Wash me clean of this guilt. Scrub me down to the bone of my wrongdoing.

[3] I know what I did. It's in front of me, day and night. [4] Against you — you alone — I sinned. I did this evil right in front of your eyes. So when you speak, your verdict stands. When you judge, you are clear. [5] I came into this world bent. From the moment my mother carried me, the crookedness was already there. [6] But you — you want truth where no one looks. You want wisdom buried in the secret places.

[7] Take hyssop to me. Make me clean. Wash me, and I will outshine snow. [8] Let me hear gladness again. Let me hear joy. Let the bones you crushed get up and dance. [9] Look away from my sins. Erase every stain.

[10] God — create in me a clean heart. Build something new. Plant a spirit inside me that won't quit. [11] Don't throw me out of your presence. Don't strip your holy Spirit from me. [12] Give me back the joy of your rescue. Steady me with a willing spirit.

[13] Then I will teach the rebels your ways. The lost will find their way home. [14] Pull me out from under this blood-guilt, God — the God who saves me — and my tongue will sing your justice out loud. [15] Open my lips, Lord, and my mouth will pour out your praise.

[16] You don't want sacrifice. I'd bring it if you did. Burnt offerings leave you cold. [17] What God wants is a broken spirit. A shattered, humbled heart — that, God, you will not turn away.

[18] Be good to Zion. Build up Jerusalem's walls. [19] Then the right sacrifices will come — offerings whole and burning, bulls climbing your altar in smoke.

[1] Why brag about evil, you so-called strongman? God's loyal love holds steady all day long.

[2] Your tongue plots ruin — a razor honed sharp, a craftsman of lies.

[3] You love wrong more than right, falsehood more than honest speech.

[4] You devour every word that tears people down, you treacherous tongue.

[5] So God will pull you down — for good. He'll snatch you up and rip you from your tent, uproot you from the land of the living. Done.

[6] The righteous will see it and a chill of awe will run through them. Then they'll laugh:

[7] "There — that's the man who refused to make God his stronghold. Trusted instead in his bottomless wealth, drew his strength from destruction."

[8] But me? I'm a green olive tree thriving in the house of God. I trust the loyal love of God — always, without end.

[9] I will thank you forever, for what you've done. I'll wait on your name — it's good — surrounded by the faithful.

[1] The fool tells himself, "There is no God." Their lives prove it — corruption, twisted deeds, not one of them doing what's right.

[2] God leans down from heaven, scanning the human race, looking for anyone with sense, anyone reaching for him.

[3] All of them — turned away. Every last one rotten through. No one doing good. Not a single one.

[4] Do these evildoers understand nothing? They devour my people like bread and never once call on God.

[5] Then terror hits them — terror out of nowhere, terror with no cause. God scatters the bones of those who came against you. You put them to shame because God rejected them.

[6] Let rescue for Israel break out of Zion! When God brings his people home from exile, Jacob will sing, Israel will dance.

[1] God, save me by your name. Vindicate me by your power.

[2] God, hear my prayer. Listen to the words leaving my mouth.

[3] Strangers have risen against me. Ruthless men hunt my life — men who give God no thought at all.

[4] But look — God is the one helping me. The Lord is the one holding my life up.

[5] Let the evil come back on those watching me. In your faithfulness, silence them.

[6] I will bring you an offering, glad and willing. I will give thanks to your name, Lord, because your name is good.

[7] You have pulled me clear of every trouble. My eyes have seen my enemies broken.

[1] God, listen to my prayer. Don't turn away when I beg you. [2] Pay attention to me. Answer. I'm pacing in my anguish, undone— [3] the enemy's voice in my ears, the wicked bearing down. They dump their malice on me and rage at me to my face.

[4] My heart twists inside me. The terror of death has settled on my shoulders. [5] Fear and trembling wash through me. Horror has buried me alive. [6] I keep saying: if only I had wings like a dove— I'd fly away. I'd find somewhere to rest. [7] I'd flee to the far country. I'd camp out in the wilderness. [8] I'd race for shelter from this howling wind, this storm.

[9] Confuse them, Lord. Split their tongues. I see violence and strife stalking the city. [10] Day and night they patrol the walls. Inside her, trouble and ruin. [11] Destruction works the streets. Oppression and deceit never leave the marketplace.

[12] If it were an enemy mocking me, I could handle it. If it were someone who hated me strutting around in triumph, I could hide. [13] But it's you. A man like me. My friend. My familiar. [14] We used to share secrets. We walked into God's house together in the crowd.

[15] Let death ambush them. Let them drop alive into the grave— because evil lives where they live, right inside them.

[16] But me—I call on God. The LORD will save me. [17] Evening, morning, noon—I cry out, I groan, and he hears my voice. [18] He buys back my life from the battle. He brings me out in peace, even though they came at me in swarms. [19] God—enthroned forever—will hear and break them, because they never change, never fear him.

[20] My friend raised his hand against those at peace with him. He violated the covenant. [21] His mouth was smooth as butter while war filled his heart. His words ran softer than oil—and every one was a drawn sword.

[22] Throw your burden on the LORD. He'll hold you up. He never lets the righteous collapse.

[23] But you, God—you'll drag the bloodthirsty and the liars down into the pit. They won't see half their days. As for me—I trust you.

[1] Have mercy on me, God — they're hunting me down. All day long the attackers press in, crushing me.

[2] My enemies trample me all day; there are so many of them, fighting from their high ground.

[3] When fear rises, I anchor myself in you.

[4] In God — whose word I praise — in God I've placed my trust. I will not be afraid. What can mere flesh do to me?

[5] All day they twist my words. Every thought they have toward me is poison.

[6] They gather, they crouch, they shadow my every step, waiting to take my life.

[7] After all this evil — should they escape? God, in your fury, bring the nations down.

[8] You've kept count of every restless night. Collect my tears in your bottle — aren't they written in your book?

[9] The day I call out, my enemies will turn and run. This I know: God is for me.

[10] In God — whose word I praise — in the Lord — whose word I praise —

[11] in God I've placed my trust. I will not be afraid. What can a human being do to me?

[12] God, the vows I made to you stand. I'll bring you offerings of thanks.

[13] Because you pulled me back from death, kept my feet from stumbling — so I could walk before God in the light of the living.

[1] Mercy, God — mercy. I'm running to you. I'm hiding in the shadow of your wings until the storm blows past.

[2] I cry out to God Most High, to the God who finishes what he starts for me.

[3] He'll reach down from heaven and save me — he'll shame the ones hunting me down. God will send out his loyal love and his truth like couriers.

[4] I'm lying down in the middle of lions — among predators with teeth like spears and arrows, tongues like sharpened blades.

[5] Rise up, God, over the heavens. Let your glory cover the whole earth.

[6] They spread a net for my feet — I was bent low. They dug a pit in my path — and fell into it themselves.

[7] My heart is steady, God. My heart is steady. I'm going to sing. I'm going to make music.

[8] Wake up, my soul. Wake up, harp and lyre. I'll wake the dawn itself.

[9] I'll thank you out loud, Lord, in front of the nations. I'll sing your praises where every people can hear.

[10] Because your loyal love rises higher than the heavens, your faithfulness reaches the clouds.

[11] Rise up, God, over the heavens. Let your glory cover the whole earth.

[1] Do you really speak justice, you so-called gods? Do you judge people fairly, you sons of men?

[2] No — in your hearts you forge corruption. Your hands measure out violence across the land.

[3] The wicked are off-course from the womb. Liars from the moment they draw breath.

[4] Their venom is snake venom — a cobra gone deaf,

[5] one that refuses to hear the charmer's voice, no matter how skilled the spell.

[6] God — shatter their teeth in their mouths. Yahweh — break the fangs of these young lions.

[7] Let them drain away like water that runs off and is gone. When they aim their arrows, let them snap in their hands.

[8] Let them be like a slug dissolving as it crawls — like a stillborn child who never sees the sun.

[9] Before your pots feel the heat of the thornfire — green or burning — God will sweep them away in the storm.

[10] The righteous will see vengeance done and rejoice. They will wash their feet in the blood of the wicked.

[11] And people will say: "Yes — there is a reward for the righteous. Yes — there is a God who judges the earth."

[1] Rescue me from my enemies, my God. Lift me beyond the reach of those rising against me.

[2] Pull me out from those who deal in evil. Save me from men who thirst for blood.

[3] Look — they're crouched in ambush for my life. Ruthless men gang up on me, and I've done nothing wrong, God — no offense, no betrayal.

[4] No guilt on my hands, yet they sprint to take their positions. Wake up. Come meet me. See what's happening.

[5] You — LORD God of Armies, God of Israel — rouse yourself. Audit every nation. Show no mercy to these treacherous schemers.

[6] Every evening they return, snarling like feral dogs, prowling the city.

[7] Listen to what spills from their mouths — swords between their teeth — and they think, "Who's listening?"

[8] But you, LORD — you laugh at them. You mock every nation.

[9] My strength, I'm watching for you. God is my fortress.

[10] My faithful God comes to meet me. God will let me look down on those who hunt me.

[11] Don't kill them outright — my people would forget too quickly. Scatter them by your power. Bring them down, Lord, our shield.

[12] For the sin of their mouths, the words on their lips — let their own arrogance trap them. For the curses and lies they spew,

[13] consume them. Consume them until nothing's left. Then everyone will know: God rules over Jacob, all the way to the ends of the earth.

[14] Evening after evening they return, snarling like feral dogs, prowling the city.

[15] They roam, scavenging for food, growling if they don't get their fill.

[16] But I — I will sing of your strength. Every morning I'll belt out your faithful love. Because you've been my high tower, my refuge when everything closed in.

[17] My strength, I'll make music for you. God is my fortress, my faithful God.

[1] God, you've thrown us off. You've broken through our lines. You've burned with anger — come back to us.

[2] You made the earth tremble, ripped it open. Now heal its fractures — the whole thing is shaking apart.

[3] You've put your people through hell. You poured us a wine that left us reeling.

[4] But for those who fear you, you've raised a banner — a rallying point against the arrows flying.

[5] Save us with your right hand. Answer us. Rescue the ones you love.

[6] God has spoken from his holy place, and this is what he said: "I claim victory. I'll parcel out Shechem. I'll measure off the Valley of Succoth.

[7] Gilead is mine. Manasseh is mine. Ephraim is my helmet. Judah is my scepter.

[8] Moab? My washbasin. Edom? I'll throw my sandal on it. Philistia — shout if you dare."

[9] Who will march me into the fortified city? Who will lead me into Edom?

[10] Wasn't it you, God — you, who rejected us? You, who stopped marching with our armies?

[11] Give us help against the enemy. Human help is worthless.

[12] With God we'll do mighty things. He'll trample the ones who trample us.

[1] Hear my cry, God. Listen to my prayer.

[2] From the edge of the earth I'm calling — heart sinking, voice cracking. Lead me to the rock that rises higher than I can climb.

[3] You've been my hiding place, a fortress wall against every enemy.

[4] Let me live in your tent forever. Let me take cover beneath the shelter of your wings.

[5] You've heard my vows, God. You've handed me the inheritance reserved for those who honor your name.

[6] Add days to the king's life. Stretch his years across generations.

[7] Seat him forever in your presence. Send loyalty and truth to stand guard over him.

[8] So I'll sing your name without stopping, paying what I promised, day after day after day.

[1] My soul waits in silence for God alone. My rescue comes from him.

[2] He alone is my rock, my deliverance, my fortress on the high ground — I will not be shaken hard.

[3] How long will you all gang up on a man, hammering away at him like he's a leaning wall, a fence already buckling?

[4] They scheme to topple him from his height. Lies are their delight. Their mouths bless — their hearts curse. *Selah.*

[5] My soul, wait in silence for God alone. My hope comes from him.

[6] He alone is my rock, my deliverance, my fortress on the high ground — I will not be shaken.

[7] On God rests my rescue and my honor. He is the cliff of my strength, my refuge.

[8] Trust him, people — every moment of your life. Pour your hearts out in his presence. God is our shelter. *Selah.*

[9] Common men are a breath. Important men are a lie. Drop them on the scales together — they float up, weighing less than nothing.

[10] Don't trust extortion. Don't put your hope in stolen wealth. If riches roll in, don't let your heart latch onto them.

[11] God has said it once — twice I have heard it: power belongs to God.

[12] And to you, Lord, belongs unfailing love — for you repay each person according to what they have done.

[1] God — you are my God. I'm hunting for you from first light. My soul is parched for you, my body aches for you, like ground that's forgotten what rain feels like.

[2] So I've come looking in the sanctuary, just to see you again — your strength, your weight, your glory.

[3] Your love is better than being alive. My mouth can't stop praising you.

[4] I'll bless you while there's breath in me. I'll lift my hands and speak your name.

[5] My soul will eat its fill — the richest meal — and my mouth will sing with joy on its lips.

[6] When I'm stretched out on my bed, I think of you. Through every watch of the night, I turn you over in my mind.

[7] Because you've been my help. In the shadow of your wings I sing.

[8] My soul clings to you. Your right hand has me.

[9] But those who hunt my life to destroy it — they're going down, deep into the earth.

[10] Handed over to the sword. Carrion for jackals.

[11] But the king will rejoice in God. Everyone who swears by him will boast — while the mouths of liars are stopped cold.

[1] Hear me, God — listen to the cry tearing out of me. Guard my life from the terror of the enemy.

[2] Hide me from the secret huddle of the wicked, from the seething mob of evildoers.

[3] They sharpen their tongues like swords. They aim cruel words like arrows on a drawn string,

[4] firing from ambush at the innocent — striking suddenly, fearless of consequence.

[5] They egg each other on in their schemes, plotting where to plant their traps, whispering, "Who'll ever see?"

[6] They cook up injustice and call it a masterpiece. The human heart and mind — what a bottomless dark.

[7] But God draws His own bow. One arrow flies — and they crumple, struck down before they know what hit them.

[8] Their own tongues turn on them and bring them crashing. Everyone who sees it walks away shaking their head.

[9] Then awe spreads through the whole human race. They tell what God has done. They finally understand His work.

[10] The righteous will rejoice in the LORD and run to Him for cover. Every honest heart will sing.

[1] Silence itself is praise to you, God in Zion. To you the promises are paid in full.

[2] You who hear prayer — all flesh comes to you.

[3] When my guilt rises up and overwhelms me, you are the one who wipes our rebellions clean.

[4] Blessed is the one you choose, the one you draw close to live in your courts. We feast on the goodness of your house, the holiness of your temple.

[5] With staggering acts of justice you answer us, God of our rescue — hope of every far horizon, every distant sea.

[6] You set the mountains in place with your strength, belted with power.

[7] You hush the roaring oceans, the crashing of their waves, the uproar of the nations.

[8] Those living at the ends of the earth stand in awe at your signs. From sunrise to sunset, you make the world sing.

[9] You visit the land and drench it, loading it with abundance. The river of God runs full of water. You prepare the grain — yes, this is how you prepare it:

[10] soaking the furrows, leveling the ridges, softening the soil with downpours, blessing what breaks through.

[11] You crown the year with your bounty; your cart-tracks overflow with richness.

[12] The wild pastures drip with it. The hills wrap themselves in joy.

[13] The meadows clothe themselves with flocks, the valleys dress in grain — they shout, they sing.

[1] Shout to God, every corner of the earth!

[2] Sing the weight of his name. Make his praise magnificent.

[3] Tell God: "Your works are staggering. Your power is so vast that your enemies cringe and crawl to you."

[4] All the earth bends to you. They sing to you. They sing your name.

[5] Come and see what God has done — his dealings with humanity leave you breathless.

[6] He turned the sea into dry ground. They walked through the river on foot. There — right there — we celebrated him.

[7] He rules forever by raw strength. His eyes track the nations. Rebels, don't even think about rising up.

[8] Bless our God, you peoples. Let the sound of his praise carry.

[9] He keeps us among the living. He won't let our feet slip.

[10] You tested us, God. You refined us the way silver is refined.

[11] You drove us into the net. You strapped a crushing weight on our backs.

[12] You let men ride right over our heads. We went through fire. We went through water. And you brought us out into abundance.

[13] I'm coming to your house with offerings. I'm paying you everything I promised —

[14] every vow my lips made, every word my mouth spoke when I was drowning.

[15] I'll bring you fat animals, the smoke of rams, bulls and goats — the full weight of it.

[16] Come, listen, all of you who fear God, and I'll tell you what he did for me.

[17] I cried out to him with my mouth — and praise was already rising under my tongue.

[18] If I had cherished sin in my heart, the Lord would not have listened.

[19] But God listened. He heard the sound of my prayer.

[20] Blessed be God — he didn't turn my prayer away, didn't pull his love back from me.

[1] God, show us favor. Bless us. Let your face blaze upon us—

[2] so the earth learns your way, so every nation tastes your saving power.

[3] Let the peoples praise you, God. Let all of them praise you.

[4] Let the nations sing, wild with joy—because you judge the peoples straight and you steer the nations of the earth. *Selah.*

[5] Let the peoples praise you, God. Let all of them praise you.

[6] The land has yielded its harvest. God—our God—is blessing us.

[7] God is blessing us. And the ends of the earth will bow in awe before him.

[1] Let God rise up. Watch his enemies scatter. Let those who hate him flee his face.
[2] Like smoke driven off by wind, drive them away. Like wax melting in fire, let the wicked dissolve before God.
[3] But the righteous — let them celebrate. Let them dance before God, wild with joy.
[4] Sing to God. Make music for his name. Build a highway for the One who rides the clouds — his name is the LORD — and revel in his presence.
[5] Father to the fatherless, defender of widows — this is God in his holy dwelling.
[6] God gives the lonely a family. He marches prisoners out into freedom. But the rebellious? They stay behind, baking in the desert.
[7] God — when you marched out ahead of your people, when you strode across the wilderness —
[8] the earth shook. The skies cracked open. Sinai itself trembled before God, the God of Israel.
[9] You poured down rain in waves, God — refreshing your weary inheritance back to life.
[10] Your people settled in. From your abundance, God, you provided for the poor.
[11] The Lord gives the word, and a vast army of women carry the news:
[12] "Kings and their armies — fleeing! Fleeing!" And the women back home divide the plunder.
[13] Even those who stayed among the sheep pens — look — wings of a dove sheathed in silver, feathers shining with gold.
[14] When the Almighty scattered kings across the land, snow fell on Mount Zalmon.
[15] Mountain of God, mountain of Bashan — peaks upon peaks, mountain of Bashan —
[16] why do you glare in envy, you jagged peaks, at the mountain God chose for his throne? The LORD will live there forever.
[17] God's chariots — tens of thousands, thousands of thousands. The Lord comes from Sinai into his sanctuary.
[18] You ascended on high. You led captives in your train. You took gifts from humanity — even from rebels — so that the LORD God might dwell among us.
[19] Blessed be the Lord — day after day he hoists our burden onto his own shoulders. He is the God who saves us.
[20] Our God is a God who delivers. From the Sovereign LORD comes escape from death itself.
[21] But God will crush the skulls of his enemies — the hairy scalp of the one who keeps walking in his guilt.
[22] The Lord declared: "I will bring them back from Bashan. I will drag them up from the depths of the sea —
[23] so you can wade through your enemies' blood, so your dogs can lap up their share."
[24] They have seen your procession, God — my God, my King, moving into the sanctuary.
[25] Singers in front, musicians behind, girls with tambourines in the middle.
[26] "Bless God in the great assembly! Bless the LORD, you who flow from Israel's spring!"
[27] There — little Benjamin leading them. Princes of Judah in their throng. Princes of Zebulun. Princes of Naphtali.
[28] Your God has commanded your strength. Show your power, God — the power you have wielded for us before.
[29] From your temple above Jerusalem, kings will haul tribute to you.
[30] Rebuke the beast hiding in the reeds — that herd of bulls among the calves of the nations. Trample them until they grovel with their silver. Scatter the peoples who delight in war.
[31] Envoys will arrive from Egypt. Cush will stretch out her hands to God.
[32] Kingdoms of the earth — sing to God. Make music for the Lord.
[33] To the One who rides the ancient skies — listen — he thunders with his mighty voice.
[34] Declare the strength of God. His majesty hangs over Israel. His power fills the clouds.
[35] Awesome — that is God in his sanctuary. The God of Israel — he is the One who hands strength and power to his people. Blessed be God.

[1] Save me, God— the waters have reached my throat.

[2] I'm sinking in bottomless mud with nothing to brace against. I've drifted into deep water and the current is dragging me under.

[3] I'm worn out from crying for help. My throat is raw. My eyes have failed, scanning the horizon for my God.

[4] More people hate me without cause than I have hairs on my head. My enemies who want me silenced for nothing— they have power, they are many. I'm being forced to return what I never stole.

[5] God, you know how foolish I've been. My failures aren't hidden from you.

[6] Don't let those who hope in you be shamed because of me, Lord, Commander of armies. Don't let those who seek you be humiliated on my account, God of Israel.

[7] I'm the one taking the insults meant for you. Disgrace covers my face like a veil.

[8] My own brothers treat me like a foreigner. My mother's sons don't recognize me anymore.

[9] Zeal for your house has eaten me alive. The insults of those who insult you have landed squarely on me.

[10] When I wept and fasted, they mocked me for it.

[11] When I wore sackcloth in grief, I became a punchline.

[12] The men at the city gate gossip about me. The drunks make up songs about me.

[13] But I'm still praying to you, LORD— at the right moment, in your overflowing love, answer me with the truth of your rescue.

[14] Pull me out of this mud before I sink any further. Pull me clear of those who hate me, out of these deep waters.

[15] Don't let the current swallow me. Don't let the deep close over my head. Don't let the pit seal its mouth on me.

[16] Answer me, LORD— your love is good. Turn toward me— your mercy is vast.

[17] Don't hide your face from your servant. I'm in trouble. Answer me quickly.

[18] Come close. Redeem me. Buy me back from my enemies.

[19] You see how they shame me, how they disgrace and humiliate me. Every one of my enemies stands in front of you.

[20] Their insults have broken my heart. I'm sick from it. I looked for sympathy— there was none. I looked for someone to comfort me— I found no one.

[21] They put poison in my food. When I was thirsty, they handed me vinegar.

[22] Let their banquet table become a trap. Let their feasts become a snare.
