[23] Let their eyes go dark so they can't see. Let their backs bend permanently under the weight.

[24] Pour out your fury on them. Let your burning anger overtake them.

[25] Leave their camp empty. Let no one live in their tents.

[26] Because they pile on the one you've already struck. They gossip about the pain of those you've wounded.

[27] Add this to the charges against them. Don't let them walk into your righteousness.

[28] Erase them from the book of the living. Don't let their names be written among the righteous.

[29] But I am in pain, I am broken— let your rescue, God, lift me up high.

[30] I will sing praise to God's name. I will magnify him with thanksgiving.

[31] And this will please the LORD more than any bull, more than any sacrifice with horns and hooves.

[32] The humble will see it and celebrate. You who seek God— let your hearts come alive.

[33] Because the LORD listens to the poor. He doesn't dismiss his prisoners.

[34] Let heaven and earth praise him. Let the seas and everything swimming in them join in.

[35] Because God will rescue Zion and rebuild the cities of Judah. His people will live there, will own it.

[36] His servants' descendants will inherit it. Those who love his name will make their home there.

[1] God — rescue me. Now. Lord — get here. Hurry.

[2] Let the ones hunting my life be humiliated, sent reeling. Let the ones who relish my ruin stagger backward in disgrace.

[3] Let those who jeer "Ha! Ha!" turn on their heels, choking on their own scorn.

[4] But everyone searching for you — let them break into joy, let them celebrate you. Let the ones who love your rescue keep saying it forever: "God is huge. God is glorious."

[5] As for me — I'm broken, I'm empty. God, come fast. You're my help, you're my way out. Lord — don't make me wait.

[1] In you, LORD, I've taken cover — don't ever let me end up disgraced.

[2] You're the righteous one — rescue me, pull me out. Lean in and listen. Save me.

[3] Be the rock I keep running to, the fortress always open to me. You gave the word — save me. You're my cliff-face, my stronghold.

[4] My God, snatch me out of the grip of the wicked, out of the fist of the cruel and the corrupt.

[5] You're what I've been waiting for, Lord — my hope since I was young, my confidence from the start.

[6] I've leaned on you since the womb. You drew me out of my mother's body. You're the reason I sing.

[7] To a lot of people I look like a walking disaster — but you've been my refuge, solid and unshaken.

[8] My mouth is full of your praise — your splendor, all day long.

[9] Don't toss me aside when I'm old. Don't walk away when my strength runs out.

[10] My enemies are talking about me. The ones plotting my death are huddled together, scheming.

[11] "God has dropped him," they say. "Go after him. Grab him. Nobody's coming to save him."

[12] God — don't stay far off. My God — hurry. Help me.

[13] Let the ones hunting me crash and burn. Let the ones who want me ruined be buried in their own shame and disgrace.

[14] But me — I'll keep hoping. I'll pile praise on top of praise.

[15] My mouth will tell the story of your righteousness, your saving acts, all day long — though the count is beyond me.

[16] I'll come in the strength of the Lord GOD. I'll tell of your righteousness — yours alone.

[17] God, you taught me from the time I was a boy, and to this day I keep announcing your wonders.

[18] Even now — gray-haired, old — don't abandon me, God. Let me tell the next generation about your arm, the coming generation about your power.

[19] Your righteousness, God, reaches the sky. You've done staggering things. Who is there like you?

[20] You've shown me trouble — mountains of it, deep and bitter — but you'll bring me back to life. You'll haul me up from the depths of the earth.

[21] You'll lift me higher. You'll come around again and comfort me.

[22] So I'll thank you with the harp — your faithfulness, my God. I'll make music to you with the lyre, Holy One of Israel.

[23] My lips will erupt in song when I make music to you — and my soul, which you bought back, will sing too.

[24] All day my tongue will repeat your righteousness — because the ones who wanted me destroyed have been shamed and humiliated.

[1] God, give the king your judgment. Give the king's son your justice. [2] Let him govern your people with righteousness, your afflicted ones with justice. [3] Let the mountains carry peace to the people, and the hills, righteousness. [4] Let him defend the cause of the poor, rescue the children of the needy, and crush the oppressor.

[5] Let them fear you as long as the sun endures, as long as the moon, through every generation. [6] Let him come down like rain on a mown field, like showers soaking the earth. [7] In his days let righteousness bloom and peace flood the land — until the moon is no more.

[8] Let his rule stretch from sea to sea, from the River to the ends of the earth. [9] Let desert tribes bow before him, his enemies lick the dust. [10] Let the kings of Tarshish and the distant coasts bring tribute. Let the kings of Sheba and Seba haul in their gifts. [11] Let every king fall down before him. Let every nation serve him.

[12] Because he rescues the needy when they cry — the afflicted, the one with no one to help. [13] He bends low over the weak and the poor. He saves the lives of those teetering at the edge. [14] He buys their lives back from violence and crushing weight. Their blood is precious in his sight.

[15] Long live the king! Let gold from Sheba be poured at his feet. Let prayer rise for him without ceasing, blessing surround him all day long. [16] Let grain flourish across the land, even on the mountain peaks. Let the harvest wave like the cedars of Lebanon. Let the cities teem with life like grass springing from the soil. [17] Let his name endure forever, outlast the sun. Let every nation find blessing in him. Let all peoples call him blessed.

[18] Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel — the only one who works wonders. [19] Blessed be his glorious name forever. Let his glory flood the whole earth. Amen and amen.

[20] Here end the prayers of David, son of Jesse.

[1] Yes — God is good to Israel, good to those whose hearts are clean.

[2] But me? My feet nearly gave way. I came that close to slipping.

[3] Because I envied the arrogant. I watched the wicked thrive and something in me broke.

[4] They don't struggle. No agony in their dying — their bodies stay sleek and strong.

[5] They skip the troubles the rest of us drown in. They don't get hit like ordinary people.

[6] So pride drapes them like a necklace. Violence fits them like a tailored coat.

[7] Their eyes bulge from rich living. Their hearts overflow with whatever they imagine.

[8] They sneer. They talk cruelty. From their high perch, they threaten everyone below.

[9] Their mouths set themselves against heaven, and their tongues swagger through the earth.

[10] So the crowd turns to them — drinks down their words like water and asks for more.

[11] They say, "How would God know? Does the Most High even notice?"

[12] Look at them — the wicked, untroubled, piling up wealth.

[13] So what was the point of keeping my heart pure? Of washing my hands clean of guilt?

[14] All day long I've been struck down. Every morning a fresh punishment.

[15] If I had said out loud what I was thinking, I would have betrayed your children.

[16] When I tried to make sense of it, the weight of it crushed me —

[17] until I walked into God's sanctuary. Then I saw how their story ends.

[18] You set them on slick ground. You're tipping them toward ruin.

[19] In an instant — gone. Swept away by terrors, every one.

[20] Like a dream when you wake — Lord, when you rise, you'll dismiss them like phantoms.

[21] When my heart turned bitter, when I was pierced through —

[22] I was the brute. The idiot. An animal standing before you, uncomprehending.

[23] And still — I am always with you. You've taken hold of my right hand.

[24] You guide me with your counsel, and afterward you will gather me into glory.

[25] Who do I have in heaven but you? And on earth — I want nothing apart from you.

[26] My body fails. My heart fails. But God is the rock of my heart, my portion forever.

[27] Look — those who drift from you perish. You finish off everyone who plays you false.

[28] But me? Being close to God is the only good I know. I've made the Lord my refuge, so I can tell what he's done.

[1] God, why? Why have You thrown us out for good? Why does Your anger smoke against the sheep of Your own pasture?

[3] Walk through these endless ruins. The enemy has wrecked everything in Your sanctuary.

[4] Your foes roared in the middle of Your meeting place. They planted their banners there as trophies.

[5] They looked like men swinging axes in a thick forest —

[6] hacking down every carved panel with hatchet and hammer.

[7] They torched Your sanctuary. They dragged the dwelling place of Your name to the ground, defiled it.

[8] They said in their hearts, "Let's crush them completely." They burned down every place where God was worshiped in the land.

[13] You split the sea with Your strength. You crushed the heads of the sea-monsters in the water.

[14] You smashed Leviathan's skulls and served him up as food to the desert creatures.

[15] You cracked open springs and streams. You dried up rivers that never run dry.

[16] The day is Yours. The night is Yours. You set the moon and sun in place.

[17] You drew every border of the earth. Summer and winter — You made them both.

[18] Remember this, LORD: the enemy mocks You. A pack of fools sneers at Your name.

[19] Don't hand over the life of Your dove to the wild beasts. Don't forget the lives of Your afflicted — not forever.

[20] Look at Your covenant. The dark corners of the land are crawling with violence.

[22] Rise up, God. Plead Your own case. Remember how fools mock You all day long.

[1] We thank you, God, we thank you. Your name is close — and the stories of your wonders spill from every mouth.

[1] God is known in Judah. His name looms large in Israel.

[1] I cry out to God — full voice, no holding back. I cry out, and I'm waiting for him to hear me.

[2] When the day went dark on me, I went looking for the Lord. All night my hand stayed stretched out, refusing to drop. My soul refused every comfort offered.

[3] I think about God — and I groan. I try to pray — and my spirit goes faint.

[4] He won't let my eyes close. I'm so undone I can't even put it into words.

[5] So I start counting backward — through the old days, through years buried deep.

[6] At night I remember the song I used to sing. I sit with my own heart and interrogate it. My spirit digs and digs:

[7] Is the Lord done with me forever? Will he never look my way again?

[8] Has his unfailing love finally failed? Has the promise gone silent for good?

[9] Did God forget how to be gracious? Did anger lock the door on his mercy?

[10] And I said: this is what wounds me — the strong right hand of the Most High has changed.

[11] But I will remember. I will remember what the Lord has done. I will call back to mind your wonders from long ago.

[12] I'll think through every act of yours. I'll turn your works over in my hands like stones.

[13] God, your way is holy ground. What god is great the way our God is great?

[14] You're the God who works wonders. You flexed your strength in front of the nations and they saw it.

[15] With one arm you bought your people back — the children of Jacob and Joseph.

[16] The waters saw you, God. The waters saw you and writhed. The deep itself shook to its core.

[17] Clouds emptied themselves like buckets. The sky cracked open with thunder. Your arrows shot in every direction.

[18] Your thunder rolled like chariots. Lightning lit up the world. The earth trembled and bucked.

[19] Your path cut straight through the sea. Your road ran across deep water. And no one could find your footprints afterward.

[20] You led your people like a flock — Moses and Aaron, the shepherds at the front.

[1] Listen, my people, to what I'm teaching. Lean in close to the words from my mouth.

[2] I'm opening my mouth in story and saying — I'm cracking open riddles buried since the beginning,

[4] We won't hide it from their children. We'll tell the next generation the praises of the LORD, his power, the wonders he worked.

[5] He set a testimony in Jacob, planted a Torah in Israel — commanded our fathers to teach it to their children,

[7] so they would put their hope in God and not forget what he's done, but keep his commands.

[8] So they wouldn't be like their fathers — a generation stubborn and defiant, hearts that wouldn't hold steady, spirits that wouldn't stay faithful to God.

[9] The men of Ephraim, armed, carrying bows — they turned and ran on the day of battle.

[10] They didn't keep God's covenant. They refused to walk in his Torah.

[11] They forgot what he'd done — the wonders he'd shown them.

[12] In front of their fathers he worked the impossible, in Egypt, in the fields of Zoan.

[13] He split the sea and walked them through. He stacked the waters up like walls.

[14] He led them by a cloud in the day, by a fire's glow all through the night.

[15] He cracked open rocks in the wilderness and gave them drink as deep as the deep itself.

[16] He pulled streams out of stone — water cascading down like rivers.

[17] But still they kept on sinning against him — defying the Most High in the desert.

[18] They tested God in their hearts, demanding the food they were craving.

[19] They spoke against God: "Can God set a table out here in the wilderness?

[20] Sure, he struck the rock and water gushed and torrents flooded — but can he hand us bread too? Can he get meat for his people?"

[21] The LORD heard. Fury erupted. Fire blazed against Jacob. Wrath rose against Israel —

[22] because they didn't believe in God. They didn't trust his power to save.

[23] So he commanded the skies above. He threw open the doors of heaven.

[24] He rained manna down on them to eat — gave them grain straight from heaven.

[25] Mortals ate the bread of angels. He sent them food until they were stuffed.

[26] He stirred up the east wind in the heavens, drove the south wind by his strength.

[27] He rained meat on them like dust, birds with wings like the sand of the sea.

[28] He dropped them right in the middle of camp, all around their tents.

[29] They ate and gorged themselves — he gave them exactly what they wanted.

[30] But before they'd even swallowed their craving — while the food was still in their mouths —

[31] the wrath of God rose up against them. He cut down their strongest men. He felled the choice young men of Israel.

[32] And still — after all this — they kept sinning. They didn't believe his wonders.

[33] So he ended their days in futility, their years in sudden terror.

[34] When he killed them, they'd come looking for him — turn back, search for God in earnest.

[35] They'd remember God was their rock, the Most High God their redeemer.

[36] But it was just lips. They flattered him with their mouths. They lied to him with their tongues.

[37] Their hearts weren't fixed on him. They weren't faithful to his covenant.

[38] But he — full of mercy — covered over their guilt and didn't destroy them. Again and again he held back his anger. He didn't unleash all his wrath.

[39] He remembered they were flesh — a breath that blows past and doesn't come back.

[40] How many times they defied him in the wilderness — wounded him out there in the wasteland.

[41] Over and over they tested God. They provoked the Holy One of Israel.

[42] They didn't remember his hand — the day he ransomed them from the enemy,

[43] when he set his signs against Egypt, his wonders in the fields of Zoan.

[44] He turned their rivers to blood — they couldn't drink from their own streams.

[45] He sent swarms of flies that devoured them, frogs that ruined them.

[46] He handed their crops to the locust, their labor to the swarm.

[47] He killed their vines with hail, their sycamores with frost.

[48] He gave their cattle over to the hail, their livestock to lightning bolts.

[49] He unleashed on them the heat of his anger — fury, indignation, calamity — a delegation of destroying angels.

[50] He cleared a path for his anger. He didn't spare them from death. He handed their lives over to plague.

[51] He struck down every firstborn in Egypt — the first fruits of strength in the tents of Ham.

[52] But his own people he led out like sheep, guided them through the wilderness like a flock.

[53] He led them safely — they weren't afraid — and the sea swallowed their enemies.

[54] He brought them to his holy border, to this mountain his right hand had taken.

[55] He drove out nations before them, measured them out an inheritance with a line, settled the tribes of Israel in the tents of the conquered.

[56] But they tested God Most High and defied him. They didn't keep his statutes.

[57] They turned back — traitors like their fathers — twisted like a warped bow.

[58] They provoked him with their high places. They made him jealous with their carved gods.

[59] God heard. Fury. He utterly rejected Israel.

[60] He walked out of his tent at Shiloh — the dwelling he'd pitched among humans.

[61] He let his strength go into captivity, his glory into the hand of the enemy.

[62] He handed his people over to the sword — was furious with his own inheritance.

[63] Fire consumed their young men. Their young women had no wedding songs.

[64] Their priests fell by the sword. Their widows couldn't even weep.

[65] Then the Lord woke up — like a man waking, like a warrior sobering from wine.

[66] He drove his enemies back, broken, and shamed them forever.

[67] He rejected the tent of Joseph. He didn't choose the tribe of Ephraim.

[68] He chose the tribe of Judah — Mount Zion, the one he loved.

[69] He built his sanctuary like the heights of heaven, like the earth he founded forever.

[70] He chose David, his servant — took him from the sheep pens,

[71] brought him in from following the nursing ewes to shepherd Jacob his people, Israel his inheritance.

[72] And David shepherded them with a heart of integrity. With skillful hands, he led them.

[1] God — the nations have stormed into what belongs to you. They desecrated your holy temple. They reduced Jerusalem to rubble.

[45] You cut short the days of his youth. You wrapped him in shame. *Selah*

[46] How long, LORD? Will you stay hidden forever? Will your fury burn like fire without end?

[47] Remember how short my life is. For what nothing did you create all humanity?

[48] What man lives and never sees death? Who escapes the grip of the grave? *Selah*

[49] Where, Lord, is the steadfast love you used to show? The love you swore to David in your faithfulness?

[50] Remember, Lord—the mockery your servants endure, how I carry in my chest the contempt of all these peoples,

[51] how your enemies mock, LORD—how they mock every step of your anointed one.

[52] Blessed be the LORD forever. Amen and amen.

[1] Lord, you have been our home through every generation.

[2] Before the mountains were born, before you labored the earth and the world into being — from forever past to forever future, you are God.

[3] You turn humans back to dust with a word: "Return, children of Adam."

[4] A thousand years in your sight pass like yesterday — already gone, like a few hours of the night watch.

[5] You sweep us away like a flash flood. We are a dream that dissolves at dawn, grass that springs up at sunrise —

[6] green and rising in the morning, withered and dead by evening.

[7] Your anger consumes us. Your fury leaves us shaking.

[8] You have laid our sins out in front of you, exposed our secrets in the full light of your face.

[9] Our days drain away under your wrath. We finish our years with a sigh.

[10] Seventy years is what we get — eighty if we are strong — and even the best of them are loaded with trouble and grief. They fly past, and we are gone.

[11] Who really grasps the force of your anger? Who fears your wrath the way it deserves?

[12] Teach us, then, to count our days — so we can carry home a heart of wisdom.

[13] Come back, Lord — how long will this last? Have mercy on your servants.

[14] Fill us with your steadfast love at daybreak, and we will sing and celebrate every day we have left.

[15] Give us joy proportional to the years you afflicted us, equal to the days we tasted disaster.

[16] Let your servants see what you can do. Let our children see your splendor.

[17] May the favor of the Lord our God rest on us. Establish the work of our hands — yes, the work of our hands, make it stand.

[1] Whoever shelters in the secret place of the Most High will spend the night in the shadow of the Almighty.

[2] I say it of the LORD: He is my refuge, my fortress, my God — and I trust Him.

[3] He's the one who rescues you from the hunter's snare, from the plague that ravages.

[4] He'll cover you with His feathers; under His wings you'll find shelter. His faithfulness is armor and shield.

[5] You won't fear the terror that stalks the night, or the arrow that flies by day,

[6] or the plague creeping through the dark, or the catastrophe that lays waste at noon.

[7] A thousand may fall at your side, ten thousand at your right hand — but it won't touch you.

[8] You'll only watch. Your eyes will see the wicked get what they earned.

[9] Because you've made the LORD — my refuge — the Most High, your dwelling,

[10] no harm will overtake you, no disaster come near your tent.

[11] He'll put His angels on assignment for you, to guard you wherever you go.

[12] They'll lift you on their hands so you don't even strike your foot against a stone.

[13] You'll walk over the lion and the cobra; you'll trample the young lion and the serpent.

[14] "Because he clings to me in love, I'll deliver him. Because he knows my name, I'll lift him high.

[15] When he calls, I'll answer. I'll be with him in trouble — I'll rescue him and crown him with honor.

[16] I'll fill him with long life and let him see my salvation with his own eyes."

[1] A song for the Sabbath day.

It is a beautiful thing to thank the LORD, to sing your name, Most High—

[2] to declare your love when morning breaks and your faithfulness through the night,

[3] strings and lyre and the deep voice of the harp.

[4] You have made me glad, LORD, by what you have done. I sing because of the work of your hands.

[5] How vast your works are, LORD. How unfathomable your thoughts.

[6] The fool cannot see it. The mindless cannot grasp it.

[7] When the wicked sprout like weeds and every evildoer blossoms, it is only so they can be destroyed forever.

[8] But you, LORD—you reign on high, eternal.

[9] Look at your enemies, LORD—look at them. Your enemies vanish. Every architect of evil scattered.

[10] You have lifted my head like the horn of a wild ox. You have poured fresh oil over me.

[11] My eyes have seen my watchers fall. My ears have heard the end of those who rose against me.

[12] The righteous burst into life like the palm tree, tower like cedars on Lebanon.

[13] Rooted in the house of the LORD, they flourish in the courts of our God.

[14] Still bearing fruit in old age. Still green. Still full of sap.

[15] Declaring: the LORD is straight and true. He is my rock. There is no crookedness in him.

[1] The LORD reigns — robed in majesty, armed with strength, belted for battle. The world stands firm. It will not be shaken.

[2] Your throne was set before time began. You are from forever.

[3] The floods rise, LORD — the floods lift their voice, the floods crash and roar.

[4] Above the thunder of mighty waters, mightier than the breakers of the sea — the LORD reigns on high, mightier still.

[5] Your decrees stand unshakable. Holiness is the beauty of your house, LORD, for all the days that will ever be.

[1] God of vengeance, Yahweh — God of vengeance, blaze forth.
[2] Rise up, Judge of the earth. Give the arrogant what they have coming.
[3] How long, Yahweh — how long will the wicked party in their triumph?
[4] They spew arrogance, they swagger in their crimes, every evildoer crowing.
[5] They grind your people down, Yahweh. They torment the ones you call your own.
[6] They murder the widow and the stranger. They slaughter orphans.
[7] And they tell themselves: "Yah doesn't see. The God of Jacob isn't paying attention."

[8] Wake up, you brutes among the people. Fools — when will you wise up?
[9] The One who planted the ear — does he not hear? The One who sculpted the eye — does he not see?
[10] The One who disciplines nations — does he not correct? The One who teaches humanity knowledge —
[11] Yahweh knows exactly what people are thinking. He knows their thoughts are empty breath.

[12] Blessed is the one you discipline, Yahweh — the one you school in your Torah,
[13] giving them calm in the days of trouble, while a pit is being dug for the wicked.
[14] Yahweh will not abandon his people. He will not walk away from his inheritance.
[15] Justice will come back to its rightful place, and every honest heart will follow it home.

[16] Who will stand up for me against the wicked? Who will fight at my side against the workers of evil?
[17] If Yahweh had not been my help, my soul would already be lying in the silence of the grave.
[18] When I said, "My foot is slipping" — your faithful love, Yahweh, held me up.
[19] When anxiety raged inside me, your consolations soothed my soul.

[20] Can a corrupt throne be your partner — one that legislates injustice and calls it law?
[21] They gang up on the righteous and condemn the innocent to death.
[22] But Yahweh has become my fortress. My God is the rock where I take cover.
[23] He will turn their evil back on them. He will silence them with their own wickedness. Yahweh our God will silence them.

[1] Come — let's sing to the LORD, let's roar out joy to the Rock who saves us. [2] Let's crash into his presence with thanksgiving, hammer out music, lift our voices high. [3] Because the LORD is the great God, the King towering over every so-called god. [4] The deep places of the earth are in his hand; the mountain peaks belong to him. [5] The sea is his — he made it. His hands shaped the dry ground.

[6] Come — let's bow down, let's drop to our knees before the LORD who made us. [7] He is our God. We are the flock he tends, the sheep in his care.

Today — if you hear his voice, [8] don't go stone-cold like at Meribah, like that day in the wilderness at Massah, [9] where your ancestors put me to the test, sized me up — after watching everything I'd done. [10] For forty years I was sick of that generation. I said: they're a people whose hearts wander, who refuse to learn my ways. [11] So I swore it in my anger: they will never set foot in my rest.

[1] Sing to the LORD a song never sung before. Sing to the LORD, every corner of the earth.

[2] Sing to the LORD. Bless his name. Announce his rescue — day after day after day.

[3] Tell the nations his glory. Tell every people what he has done.

[4] The LORD is immense, and praise is the only fitting response. He is to be held in awe above every so-called god —

[5] because the gods of the nations are nothing. Empty. Hollow. The LORD made the skies.

[6] Splendor and majesty stand before him. Strength and beauty fill his sanctuary.

[7] Give to the LORD, families of the earth — give to the LORD glory and strength.

[8] Give to the LORD the glory his name deserves. Bring an offering. Walk into his courts.

[9] Bow before the LORD in sacred splendor. Tremble before him, every inch of the earth.

[10] Tell the nations: The LORD reigns. The world stands firm — it cannot be shaken. He will judge the peoples with absolute fairness.

[11] Let the skies rejoice. Let the earth break into joy. Let the sea thunder, and everything that fills it.

[12] Let the fields celebrate, and everything growing in them. Then every tree in the forest will sing for joy —

[13] sing before the LORD, because he is coming. He is coming to judge the earth. He will judge the world with righteousness, and the peoples with his truth.

[1] The LORD reigns — let the earth erupt in joy, let the farthest coastlands celebrate.

[2] Cloud and thick darkness wrap around him; righteousness and justice are the bedrock of his throne.

[3] Fire blazes ahead of him, scorching his enemies on every side.

[4] His lightning lights up the world — the earth sees it and shudders.

[5] Mountains melt like wax in his presence, before the Lord of all the earth.

[6] The skies broadcast his righteousness; every nation witnesses his glory.

[7] Shame on all who worship carved gods, who boast in worthless idols — bow down to him, every so-called god.

[8] Zion hears and rejoices, the daughters of Judah celebrate because of your verdicts, LORD.

[9] You, LORD, tower over the whole earth — lifted high above every god there is.

[10] You who love the LORD — hate evil. He guards the lives of his faithful ones, rescues them from the grip of the wicked.

[11] Light is sown for the righteous, joy planted for the upright in heart.

[12] Rejoice in the LORD, you who are righteous — give thanks to his holy name.

[1] Sing a new song to the LORD — he has done wonders. His own right hand, his holy arm, has carved out the victory.

[2] The LORD has made his rescue known; he has unveiled his justice before every watching nation.

[3] He has not forgotten — his love for Israel, his faithfulness to the house of his people. Every corner of the earth has seen what our God can do.

[4] Roar for the LORD, all the earth! Erupt — break into song — let the music tear loose!

[5] Play for the LORD on the harp, on the harp with the voice of singing,

[6] with trumpets blazing and the shofar wailing — shout in the presence of the King, the LORD!

[7] Let the sea thunder and everything in it, the world and all who live there.

[8] Let the rivers clap their hands, the mountains sing together —

[9] before the LORD, because he is coming. He is coming to judge the earth. He will judge the world with righteousness, the peoples with fairness.

[1] The LORD reigns — let the nations tremble. He sits enthroned above the cherubim — let the earth quake.

[2] The LORD is mighty in Zion, towering over every people.

[3] Let them praise your name — great, terrifying, holy.

[4] The King is strong — and He loves justice. You established what is right; in Jacob you have done what is just and fair.

[5] Lift Him high — the LORD our God. Fall down at His footstool. He is holy.

[6] Moses and Aaron stood among His priests. Samuel was among those who called on His name. They cried out to the LORD, and He answered them.

[7] He spoke to them from the pillar of cloud. They kept His decrees — the statutes He handed down.

[8] LORD our God — you answered them. You were a forgiving God to them, even as you held them accountable for what they did.

[9] Lift Him high — the LORD our God. Bow at His holy mountain. The LORD our God — He is holy.

[1] Roar your joy to the LORD, every nation, every soul. [2] Serve him with gladness. Come into his presence singing. [3] Know this — the LORD is God. He made us, and we belong to him. We are his people, the sheep he tends. [4] Walk through his gates with thanks on your lips, into his courts with praise. Thank him. Bless his name. [5] Because the LORD is good. His love never runs dry. His faithfulness holds — generation after generation, without end.

[1] I'll sing of love and justice. To you, LORD, I'll make my music.

[2] I'll walk the road of integrity—when will you come to me? I'll move through my own house with a clean heart.

[3] I'll set nothing worthless before my eyes. I despise the work of those who turn aside—it gets no grip on me.

[4] A twisted heart will keep its distance from me. Evil, I refuse to know you.

[5] Whoever slanders his neighbor in secret—I'll silence him. The proud eye, the swollen heart—I cannot stomach.

[6] My eyes search the faithful in the land; they're the ones who'll sit at my table. Whoever walks the road of integrity—that's who serves me.

[7] No deceiver lives under my roof. No liar holds his ground before my eyes.

[8] Every morning I'll silence the wicked of the land—cutting every evildoer out of the LORD's city.

[1] Hear me, LORD. Let my cry break through to you.

[2] Don't turn away when I'm drowning. Lean close. When I call, answer me — now, not later.

[3] My days dissolve like smoke. My bones burn like coals in a fire.

[4] My heart is scorched grass, withered. I forget to eat.

[5] I groan so hard my body is bone stretched over skin.

[6] I'm a desert owl in the wastelands, an abandoned owl haunting ruins.

[7] I lie awake — a single bird on a rooftop, alone.

[8] All day my enemies taunt me. Those who once mocked now curse with my name.

[9] Ashes are my bread. My drink is mixed with tears —

[10] because of your fury, your fierce wrath. You lifted me up only to fling me down.

[11] My days are a lengthening shadow. I'm grass, drying out.

[12] But you, LORD — you sit enthroned forever. Your name carries through every generation.

[13] You will rise. You will have mercy on Zion. The time has come — the appointed hour is here.

[14] Your servants love every stone of her. Even her dust moves them to tears.

[15] The nations will fear the name of the LORD. Every king on earth will tremble at your glory.

[16] Because the LORD will rebuild Zion. He will appear in his splendor.

[17] He will hear the prayer of the destitute. He will not despise their plea.

[18] Write this down for the generation not yet born — so a people still to be created will praise the LORD:

[19] He looked down from his holy heights. From heaven the LORD surveyed the earth,

[20] to hear the groans of the prisoners, to free those condemned to die.

[21] So the name of the LORD will be declared in Zion, his praise in Jerusalem,

[22] when peoples and kingdoms gather together to worship the LORD.

[23] He broke my strength mid-journey. He cut my days short.

[24] So I said: My God, don't take me in the middle of my life — you, whose years run on through every generation.

[25] Long ago you laid the earth's foundations. The heavens are the work of your hands.

[26] They will perish — you will remain. They will wear out like an old coat. You will change them like clothing, and they will be discarded.

[27] But you are the same. Your years have no end.

[28] The children of your servants will live secure. Their descendants will stand firm before you.

[1] Bless the LORD, O my soul — every fiber of me, bless his holy name.

[2] Bless the LORD, O my soul, and don't you dare forget one thing he's done —

[3] he forgives every last one of your sins, heals every disease that ravages you,

[4] hauls your life back from the pit, crowns you with steadfast love and tender mercy,

[5] fills your days with good things until your youth comes roaring back like an eagle's.

[6] The LORD deals in justice. He vindicates everyone the world has crushed.

[7] He showed Moses his ways. He let Israel see what he could do.

[8] The LORD is merciful and gracious — slow to burn, drowning in steadfast love.

[9] He will not nag forever. He does not hold his anger for keeps.

[10] He has not paid us back for our sins or settled the score for what we've done wrong.

[11] As high as the sky stretches above the earth — that is how vast his love is for those who fear him.

[12] As far as east is from west — that is how far he has hurled our sins from us.

[13] As a father aches with tenderness over his children, so the LORD aches over those who fear him.

[14] He knows what we're made of. He remembers we are dust.

[15] As for us — our days are grass. We bloom like a wildflower in a field;

[16] the wind passes over and we're gone. The place that knew us forgets we were ever there.

[17] But the steadfast love of the LORD runs from forever to forever for those who fear him, and his righteousness reaches their children's children —

[18] every soul who keeps his covenant, who remembers to live what he commanded.

[19] The LORD has set his throne in the heavens. His kingdom rules over everything.

[20] Bless the LORD, you his angels — mighty ones who carry out his word, who leap to obey the voice of his command.

[21] Bless the LORD, all his armies — every servant who does his will.

[22] Bless the LORD, every work of his hands, in every corner of his dominion. Bless the LORD, O my soul.

[1] Bless the LORD, my soul. O LORD my God, you are immense beyond telling — robed in splendor, draped in majesty,

[2] wrapped in light like a garment, stretching the heavens out like a tent over the world.

[3] You frame the rafters of your upper chambers in the deep waters, you ride the clouds like a chariot, you walk on the wings of the wind.

[4] You conscript the winds as your messengers and flames of fire as your servants.

[5] You set the earth on its foundations — it will never be moved.

[6] You covered it with the deep like a cloak; the waters stood high over the mountains.

[7] At your roar they fled, at the crack of your thunder they ran for cover —

[8] climbing the mountains, pouring down the valleys, settling into the place you assigned them.

[9] You drew a line they cannot cross. Never again will they swallow the earth.

[10] You send springs gushing through the canyons, weaving between the hills,

[11] giving drink to every wild creature; the wild donkeys quench their thirst.

[12] Birds of the air nest along the banks and sing from the branches.

[13] From your high chambers you water the mountains; the earth grows full on the fruit of your work.

[14] You grow grass for the cattle and crops for humans to cultivate, drawing food out of the ground —

[15] wine to gladden the human heart, oil to make a face shine, bread to steady the body.

[16] The trees of the LORD drink deep — the cedars of Lebanon he planted —

[17] where the birds build their nests; the stork makes her home in the cypress.

[18] High peaks belong to the wild goats; the cliffs are a refuge for the rock badgers.

[19] You made the moon to mark the seasons; the sun knows when to set.

[20] You bring down the darkness and night falls, and every forest creature stirs.

[21] The young lions roar for their prey, asking God for their food.

[22] When the sun rises they slip away and settle into their dens.

[23] Then humans head out to their work, to their labor until evening.

[24] How many things you have made, LORD — all of them in wisdom. The earth is packed with what you own.

[25] There lies the sea — vast, wide-armed — teeming with life beyond counting, creatures small and great.

[26] Ships glide across it. Leviathan plays there, the one you shaped to romp in the deep.

[27] Every one of them looks to you to feed them when the time comes.

[28] You give — they gather. You open your hand — they feast on good things.

[29] You hide your face — they panic. You take their breath — they die and return to dust.

[30] You send out your Spirit — they are created. You make the face of the earth new again.

[31] Let the glory of the LORD stand forever. Let the LORD delight in what he has made —

[32] he who glances at the earth and it trembles, who touches the mountains and they smoke.

[33] I will sing to the LORD as long as I live. I will make music to my God while I have breath.

[34] May my meditation be sweet to him. As for me, I will rejoice in the LORD.

[35] Let sinners vanish from the earth and the wicked be no more. Bless the LORD, my soul. Hallelujah.

[1] Thank the LORD. Call out his name. Tell the nations what he has done.

[2] Sing to him. Make music for him. Talk about every wonder he has worked.

[3] Take pride in his holy name. Let the hearts of those who seek the LORD be glad.

[4] Search for the LORD and his strength. Seek his face — always.

[5] Remember the wonders he has done, the signs he gave, the verdicts spoken from his mouth —

[6] you, the seed of Abraham his servant, you, the children of Jacob, his chosen.

[7] He is the LORD our God. His justice runs across the whole earth.

[8] He remembers his covenant forever — the word he commanded to a thousand generations,

[9] the covenant he cut with Abraham, the oath he swore to Isaac,

[10] the decree he set in stone for Jacob, the everlasting covenant for Israel:

[12] Back when they were nothing — a handful of strangers drifting through the land —

[13] wandering nation to nation, kingdom to kingdom,

[14] he let no one crush them. He warned off kings on their behalf:

[15] "Don't lay a finger on my anointed ones. Don't harm my prophets."

[16] Then he called down famine on the land. He shattered every staff of bread.

[17] But he had sent a man ahead of them — Joseph, sold as a slave.

[18] They clamped his feet with shackles. Iron bit into his neck

[19] until the time came when his word was vindicated, when the word of the LORD proved him true.

[20] The king sent and set him free. The ruler of nations released him.

[21] He made him master of his house, lord over everything he owned —

[22] free to bind princes as he pleased, to teach wisdom to his elders.

[23] Then Israel came down to Egypt. Jacob made his home in the land of Ham.

[24] God multiplied his people — made them stronger than their enemies.

[25] He turned the hearts of the Egyptians to hate his people, to scheme against his servants.

[26] So he sent Moses, his servant. And Aaron, the one he chose.

[27] They worked his signs among them — wonders in the land of Ham.

[28] He sent darkness, and darkness fell. Yet they would not heed his word.

[29] He turned their waters to blood. He killed their fish.

[30] Their land swarmed with frogs — even into the bedrooms of their kings.

[31] He spoke, and flies came in clouds. Gnats filled every corner of their territory.

[32] He gave them hail instead of rain, lightning ripping through their land.

[33] He smashed their vines and fig trees, splintered the trees of their borders.

[34] He spoke, and locusts came — grasshoppers beyond counting.

[35] They devoured every green thing, stripped the fruit from the soil.

[36] Then he struck down every firstborn in the land, the first sign of every man's strength.

[37] He brought his people out loaded with silver and gold. Among all their tribes, not one stumbled.

[38] Egypt was glad when they left — dread of Israel had fallen over them.

[39] He spread a cloud as a canopy, fire to light the night.

[40] They asked, and he brought quail. He filled them with bread from heaven.

[41] He split open the rock, and water gushed — a river running through the desert.

[42] Because he remembered his holy word, the promise to Abraham his servant.

[43] So he led his people out with joy, his chosen ones with shouts of triumph.

[44] He handed them the lands of nations. They inherited what other peoples had labored to build —

[45] so they would keep his statutes and guard his laws. Praise the LORD.

[1] Praise the LORD. Thank him — he is good. His love does not run out.

[36] They worshiped their idols — and the idols became a snare.

[41] He handed them over to the nations; their haters ruled them.

[42] Their enemies pressed them down, ground them under the heel.

[48] Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from forever to forever. And let all the people say: Amen. Praise the LORD.

[1] Thank the LORD — he is good. His love never quits.

[9] he satisfies the parched throat, he fills the starving belly with good things.

[10] Some sat in darkness, in the deepest shadow, prisoners chained in their misery,

[11] because they spat on the words of God and shrugged off the counsel of the Most High.

[12] So he broke their hearts with hard labor. They stumbled, and no one came to help.

[13] Then they cried to the LORD in their distress, and he rescued them.

[14] He brought them out of darkness, out of the deepest shadow — snapped their chains in two.

[15] Let them thank the LORD for his relentless love, for the wonders he works for the children of dust —

[16] he shatters bronze gates, he cuts iron bars to pieces.

[17] Some became fools through their own rebellion, suffering for the way they lived.

[18] Food turned to ash in their mouths. They stood at death's doorstep.

[19] Then they cried to the LORD in their distress, and he saved them.

[20] He sent out his word and healed them, snatched them back from the grave.

[21] Let them thank the LORD for his relentless love, for the wonders he works for the children of dust.

[22] Let them bring sacrifices of thanks. Let them shout his deeds with songs of joy.

[23] Some put out to sea in ships, doing business on the great waters.

[24] They saw what the LORD can do — his wonders rolling in the deep.

[25] He spoke, and a gale rose up, lifting the waves into mountains.

[26] The ship climbed the sky, plunged to the depths — their courage melted in the chaos.

[27] They reeled, staggered like drunks, every skill they had useless.

[28] Then they cried to the LORD in their distress, and he brought them through.

[29] He stilled the storm to a whisper; the waves of the sea went quiet.

[30] They were glad of the calm, and he steered them to the harbor they longed for.

[31] Let them thank the LORD for his relentless love, for the wonders he works for the children of dust.

[32] Let them lift him up in the assembly of the people, praise him where the elders sit.

[33] He turns rivers into desert, springs into dry ground,

[34] fertile land into salt flats — because the people living there were wicked.

[35] And he turns desert into pools, parched earth into bubbling springs.

[36] He settles the hungry there. They build a city to live in.

[37] They plant fields, plant vineyards, bring in a harvest that overflows.

[38] He blesses them, and they multiply. Their herds do not dwindle.

[39] But when they shrink, beaten down by oppression, calamity, and grief,

[40] he pours contempt on princes, sends them wandering in trackless wastes.

[41] Yet he lifts the poor out of misery, makes their families bloom like flocks.

[42] The upright see it and rejoice; every mouth of injustice slams shut.

[43] Whoever is wise, pay attention to this — sit with the steadfast love of the LORD.

[1] My heart is steady, God — steady and ready. I'll sing. I'll make music with everything in me.

[1] God of my praise, do not stay silent.

[2] Their mouths against me are wicked and treacherous — tongues spitting lies,
[3] words of hate surround me, attacks unprovoked.
[4] I love them, and they accuse me. But I — I am prayer.

[1] Praise! Servants of the LORD, praise. Praise the name of the LORD.

[1] I love the LORD — because he heard me, because he listened when I begged for mercy. [2] He bent down to catch my voice, so I will call on him as long as I have breath.

[7] Peace inside your walls. Security inside your towers.

[8] For the sake of my family, for the sake of my friends, I will say it: peace be on you.

[9] For the sake of the house of the LORD our God, I will seek your good.

[1] I lift my eyes to you, the One enthroned in the heavens.

[2] Look — as a servant's eyes track the hand of his master, as a maid's eyes follow the hand of her mistress, so our eyes stay fixed on the LORD our God, waiting for him to show us mercy.

[3] Mercy, LORD. Mercy. We have swallowed contempt until we're sick with it.

[4] Our souls are stuffed to the throat with the mockery of the comfortable, the scorn of the arrogant.

[1] If the LORD had not been on our side — let Israel say it now —
[2] if the LORD had not been on our side when men rose up against us,
[3] they would have swallowed us alive, their fury blazing hot enough to consume us whole.
[4] The floodwaters would have swept us away, the torrent dragging us under,
[5] the raging waters closing over our heads.
[6] Blessed be the LORD, who did not hand us over as prey for their teeth.
[7] We escaped like a bird from the hunter's snare — the snare snapped, and we flew free.
[8] Our help is in the name of the LORD, the One who made heaven and earth.

[1] Trust the LORD and you become Mount Zion — unshaken, unmoved, standing forever.

[2] The mountains wrap around Jerusalem, and the LORD wraps around his people — now and always.

[3] The scepter of the wicked will not rest on the land given to the righteous, or the righteous themselves might reach for crooked things.

[4] Be good, LORD, to those who are good, to the ones whose hearts run straight.

[5] But those who veer off into twisted paths — the LORD will march them out with the evildoers.

Peace on Israel.

[1] When the LORD brought Zion's exiles home, we were like people walking through a dream. [2] Our mouths filled with laughter, our tongues with shouts of joy. The nations said it themselves: "The LORD has done massive things for them." [3] The LORD has done massive things for us — and we are wrecked with joy.

[4] Do it again, LORD. Bring back what was lost, like desert riverbeds suddenly running with water. [5] Those who sow in tears will reap with shouts of joy. [6] The one who walks out weeping, carrying the seed to scatter, will come back singing, arms full of harvest.

[1] If the LORD doesn't build the house, the builders are just stacking stones. If the LORD doesn't guard the city, the watchman might as well sleep through his shift.

[2] You think it helps — getting up before dawn, staying up past midnight, gnawing on bread you earned by grinding yourself down? He gives rest to those he loves while they sleep.

[3] Children are a gift straight from the LORD. The womb's harvest is his reward.

[4] Sons born when you're young are arrows in a warrior's hand —

[5] and the man whose quiver is full of them is blessed. He won't be shamed when he stands toe-to-toe with his enemies at the gate.

[1] Blessed is everyone who fears the LORD, who walks the road he marks out.

[2] You will eat what your own hands have earned. You will flourish. Things will go well with you.

[3] Your wife — a vine heavy with fruit in the heart of your home. Your children — young olive shoots crowding around your table.

[4] This is the blessing that falls on the one who fears the LORD.

[5] May the LORD bless you from Zion. May you watch Jerusalem prosper every day of your life.

[6] May you live to see your children's children. Peace on Israel.

[1] They have hammered me from my youth — let Israel say it now —
[2] hammered me from my youth, but never broken me.
[3] They plowed across my back, dragging long furrows through my flesh.
[4] But the LORD is just. He cut the ropes the wicked had me bound with.

[5] Let every hater of Zion stagger backward in shame.
[6] Let them be the grass that sprouts on a rooftop — withered before it ever grows tall,
[7] never enough to fill a reaper's hand or bind into a sheaf,
[8] never enough for travelers to pause and say, "The LORD's blessing on you — we bless you in the name of the LORD."

[1] From the depths I'm calling out to you, Lord. [2] Lord, hear my voice. Let your ears tune in to the sound of my pleading.

[3] If you kept a ledger of sins, Lord — who could stand? [4] But forgiveness is yours to give. That's why you're feared.

[5] I wait for the Lord. My soul waits. And in his word I put my hope. [6] My soul watches for the Lord more than night watchmen wait for morning — more, far more, than watchmen wait for morning.

[7] Israel, hope in the Lord. With him there is unfailing love, with him there is rescue without limit. [8] He himself will redeem Israel from every last sin.

[1] LORD, my heart isn't proud. My eyes don't look down on anyone. I don't chase after things too big for me, wonders beyond my reach. [2] No — I've quieted myself, stilled my soul, like a child resting against its mother, no longer crying for the breast. That's my soul. Calm. Content. [3] Israel — put your hope in the LORD. Now. Always.

[1] LORD, remember David — remember everything he endured,

[2] how he swore to the LORD, made his vow to the Mighty One of Jacob:

[3] "I will not step inside my house, will not climb into my bed,

[4] will not give my eyes sleep or my eyelids even a moment's rest,

[5] until I find a place for the LORD, a dwelling for the Mighty One of Jacob."

[6] We heard the ark was in Ephrathah; we tracked it down in the fields of Jaar.

[7] "Let's go to his dwelling place. Let's fall down at his footstool."

[8] Rise up, LORD — come to your resting place, you and the ark of your strength.

[9] Clothe your priests in righteousness. Let your faithful ones shout for joy.

[10] For the sake of David your servant, do not turn away from your anointed king.

[11] The LORD swore an oath to David — a solid oath, one he will not take back: "I will set one of your own sons on your throne.

[12] If your descendants keep my covenant and the decrees I teach them, then their sons too will sit on your throne — forever."

[13] For the LORD has chosen Zion. He wanted it for his home:

[14] "This is my resting place forever. Here I will sit enthroned — I wanted it.

[15] I will bless this city with abundance; I will fill its poor with bread.

[16] I will clothe its priests in salvation, and its faithful ones will sing — they will sing out loud.

[17] Here I will make a horn sprout for David. I have prepared a lamp for my anointed.

[18] His enemies I will clothe in shame — but on him, the crown will blaze."

[1] Look at this — how good, how beautiful, when family lives as one. [2] It's oil poured over the head, running down — down Aaron's beard, soaking into the collar of his robes. [3] It's the dew of Hermon falling on the hills of Zion. Because that's where the LORD pours out his blessing — life that doesn't end.

[1] Now—bless the LORD, all you servants of the LORD, you who stand the night watch in the house of the LORD.

[2] Lift your hands toward the sanctuary and bless him.

[3] May the LORD bless you from Zion— he who made heaven and earth.

[1] Praise. Praise the name of the LORD. Lift him up, you who serve him—

[2] you who stand in the house of the LORD, in the courts where our God lives.

[3] Praise the LORD, because the LORD is good. Make music to his name, because it is beautiful.

[4] The LORD chose Jacob for himself. Israel—his own treasure.

[5] I know this in my bones: the LORD is great. Our Lord towers above every god.

[6] Whatever the LORD wants, he does— in heaven, on earth, in the seas, in every deep place.

[7] He hauls clouds up from the ends of the earth. He turns rain into lightning. He releases the wind from his storerooms.

[8] He struck down Egypt's firstborn— from human to livestock, all of them.

[9] He sent signs and wonders into your heart, Egypt— against Pharaoh and everyone serving him.

[10] He cut down whole nations, killed mighty kings—

[11] Sihon, king of the Amorites, Og, king of Bashan, and every kingdom in Canaan—

[12] then handed their land over as an inheritance, an inheritance for Israel, his people.

[13] LORD, your name lasts forever. LORD, your renown runs through every generation.

[14] The LORD will vindicate his people. He will have compassion on those who serve him.

[15] The idols of the nations? Silver and gold. Things human hands made.

[16] Mouths that cannot speak. Eyes that cannot see.

[17] Ears that cannot hear. No breath in their lungs.

[18] Those who make them become them. So does everyone who trusts them.

[19] House of Israel, bless the LORD. House of Aaron, bless the LORD.

[20] House of Levi, bless the LORD. You who fear the LORD, bless the LORD.

[21] Blessed be the LORD from Zion— the One who makes his home in Jerusalem. Praise the LORD.

[1] Thank the LORD — he is good. His love never quits.
[2] Thank the God above all gods. His love never quits.
[3] Thank the Lord above all lords. His love never quits.

[4] He alone works wonders that stop the breath. His love never quits.
[5] He framed the heavens with wisdom. His love never quits.
[6] He stretched the earth out over the deep. His love never quits.
[7] He hung the great lights in the sky. His love never quits.
[8] The sun to govern the day. His love never quits.
[9] The moon and stars to govern the night. His love never quits.

[10] He struck down Egypt's firstborn. His love never quits.
[11] He pulled Israel out from under them. His love never quits.
[12] With a fist of iron and an arm that would not be denied. His love never quits.
[13] He split the Red Sea in two. His love never quits.
[14] He marched Israel straight through the gap. His love never quits.
[15] He dumped Pharaoh and his army into the surf. His love never quits.

[16] He led his people through the wilderness. His love never quits.
[17] He cut down kings of staggering power. His love never quits.
[18] He killed kings whose names made nations tremble. His love never quits.
[19] Sihon, king of the Amorites. His love never quits.
[20] Og, king of Bashan. His love never quits.
[21] He handed their land over as an inheritance. His love never quits.
[22] An inheritance for Israel, his servant. His love never quits.

[23] When we were flat on our backs, he remembered us. His love never quits.
[24] He tore us free from our enemies. His love never quits.
[25] He feeds every living thing. His love never quits.

[26] Thank the God of heaven. His love never quits.

[1] By the rivers of Babylon — that's where we sat down and wept, remembering Zion.

[2] We hung our harps in the willow branches.

[3] Because there our captors demanded songs from us, our tormentors called for joy: "Sing us one of those Zion songs!"

[4] How? How do we sing the LORD's song on foreign soil?

[5] Jerusalem — if I forget you, let my right hand forget what it knows.

[6] Let my tongue stick to the roof of my mouth if I stop remembering you, if I don't lift Jerusalem above my highest joy.

[7] LORD, don't forget what the Edomites did the day Jerusalem fell — how they shouted, "Tear it down! Tear it down to the foundations!"

[8] Daughter Babylon, you destroyer — blessed is the one who pays you back what you've done to us.

[9] Blessed is the one who seizes your infants and dashes them against the rocks.

[1] I thank you with everything in me. In front of the gods themselves, I sing to you.

[2] I bow toward your holy sanctuary and praise your name for your steady love, for your unwavering faithfulness — because you have lifted your word and your name above all else.

[3] The day I called, you answered. You poured strength into my soul and made me bold.

[4] Every king on earth will praise you, LORD, when they hear what comes from your mouth.

[5] They will sing of your ways — how vast the glory of the LORD.

[6] He sits exalted, yet sees the lowly. The proud he knows from a distance.

[7] When I walk straight into the storm, you keep me alive. You stretch your hand against the rage of my enemies, and your right hand pulls me through.

[8] The LORD will finish what he started for me. Your love, LORD, never quits — do not abandon the work of your hands.

[1] Lord, you have searched me. You know me.

[2] You know when I sit down and when I rise. You read my thoughts from a thousand miles away.

[3] You map my travels and my rest. Every path I take, you know it by heart.

[4] Before a word forms on my tongue, Lord — you already know the whole of it.

[5] You hem me in, behind and before. You lay your hand on me.

[6] This kind of knowing is too much for me. It towers over me. I cannot reach it.

[7] Where could I go to escape your Spirit? Where could I run that your face would not find me?

[8] If I climb to the heavens — you are there. If I make my bed in the grave — there you are again.

[9] If I catch the wings of the dawn and fly to the far side of the sea —

[10] even there, your hand would steer me. Your right hand would hold me fast.

[11] If I said, "Let the darkness swallow me, let night close over me like a lid" —

[12] even darkness would not be dark to you. Night blazes like noon. To you, dark and light are the same thing.

[13] You shaped my inmost being. You knit me together inside my mother.

[14] I praise you — I am made fearfully, wondrously. Your works are staggering, and my soul knows it through and through.

[15] My frame was not hidden from you when I was being formed in secret, woven together in the depths of the earth.

[16] Your eyes saw me as an unfinished thing. Every one of my days was written in your book before a single one of them began.

[17] How precious your thoughts are to me, God — how vast the sum of them.

[18] If I tried to count them, they would outnumber the grains of sand. I wake — and still I am with you.

[19] God, if only you would put the wicked to death. You men of blood — get away from me.

[20] They speak of you with malice. Your enemies lift your name in vain.

[21] Lord, don't I hate those who hate you? Don't I loathe those who rise against you?

[22] I hate them with a hatred complete. I count them as enemies of my own.

[23] Search me, God. Know my heart. Test me. Know my anxious thoughts.

[24] See if there is any path in me that wounds — and lead me on the road that lasts forever.

[1] Rescue me, LORD, from violent men. Guard me from those who feed on cruelty—

[2] who weave malice in the dark of their hearts, who stir up war every waking hour.

[3] Their tongues are honed like a serpent's; viper's venom waits behind their lips. *Selah*

[4] Shield me, LORD, from the grip of the wicked. Guard me from violent men plotting to knock my feet from under me.

[5] The arrogant have hidden a snare in my path, stretched out a net beside the road, set traps where I walk. *Selah*

[6] I said to the LORD: *You are my God.* Hear me, LORD—hear my voice crying for mercy.

[7] LORD God, my strong deliverer—you shielded my head on the day of battle.

[8] Do not give the wicked what they crave, LORD. Do not let their schemes succeed, or they will only swell with pride. *Selah*

[9] As for those who circle me—let the malice on their lips come crashing down on their own heads.

[10] Let burning coals rain on them. Let them be hurled into the fire, into pits so deep they never climb out.

[11] Let no slanderer find a foothold in the land. Let evil hunt the violent man down and drive him to ruin.

[12] I know this: the LORD takes up the cause of the afflicted. He brings justice to the poor.

[13] The righteous will speak your name in praise. The upright will live in the light of your face.

[1] Lord, I'm calling — come quickly. Hear my voice when I cry out to you.

[2] Let my prayer rise before you like incense, my lifted hands like the evening offering.

[3] Set a guard at my mouth, Lord. Post a sentry at the door of my lips.

[4] Don't let my heart drift toward evil, don't let me dabble in wickedness with men who traffic in it — don't let me taste their delicacies.

[5] Let the righteous strike me — that's kindness. Let them correct me — that's oil for my head; my head won't refuse it. My prayer keeps standing against the evil they do.

[6] When their rulers are hurled down the cliffs, then people will hear my words and know they were true.

[7] Our bones lie scattered at the mouth of the grave, like earth split open and broken by the plow.

[8] But my eyes stay fixed on you, Lord — Sovereign Lord, you are my refuge. Don't leave me defenseless.

[9] Keep me clear of the traps they've laid for me, the snares of those who deal in evil.

[10] Let the wicked fall into their own nets while I walk past untouched.

[1] I cry out to the LORD with my voice. With my voice I plead for mercy.

[2] I pour out my complaint before him. I lay my trouble bare in his presence.

[3] When my spirit collapses inside me, you are the one who knows the path I walk. On the road I travel, they have hidden a trap for me.

[4] Look right and see — no one notices me. Every refuge has vanished. No one cares whether I live.

[5] So I cry out to you, LORD. I say: You are my shelter, my portion in the land of the living.

[6] Listen to my cry — I am stripped down to nothing. Rescue me from those hunting me down. They are stronger than I am.

[7] Break me out of this prison so I can give thanks to your name. The righteous will gather around me when you deal generously with me.

[1] LORD, hear my prayer. Let my cry for mercy reach you. In your faithfulness, in your righteousness, answer me.

[2] Do not put your servant on trial — no one alive comes out righteous before you.

[3] The enemy is hunting me down. He has ground my life into the dirt, locked me in darkness like the long-dead.

[4] My spirit is failing inside me. My heart sits in ruins.

[5] So I remember. I think back to the old days, turn over everything you have done, replay the work of your hands.

[6] I stretch my hands out to you. My soul thirsts for you like cracked, exhausted ground.

[7] Answer me fast, LORD — my spirit is going under. Do not turn your face away, or I will be one more body in the pit.

[8] Let morning bring word of your steady love — I am betting everything on you. Show me the road I should walk; I lift my soul to you.

[9] Rescue me, LORD, from my enemies — I run to you for cover.

[10] Teach me to do what you want, because you are my God. Let your good Spirit walk me onto level ground.

[11] For your name's sake, LORD, keep me alive. In your righteousness, pull my soul out of this trouble.

[12] In your unfailing love, silence my enemies. Wipe out every adversary closing in on me — I am yours.

[1] Blessed be the LORD, my rock—
the one who trains my hands for war,
my fingers for the fight.

[2] My steadfast love, my fortress,
my stronghold, my deliverer,
my shield, the one I run to—
who bends nations beneath me.

[3] LORD, what is a human being that you notice him?
What is mortal flesh that you give it a thought?

[4] We are vapor.
Our days—a shadow sliding past.

[5] LORD, tear the heavens open and come down.
Touch the mountains—watch them smoke.

[6] Hurl your lightning—scatter them.
Loose your arrows—send them reeling.

[7] Reach down from the heights.
Pull me out, rescue me
from the deep waters,
from the grip of foreigners

[8] whose mouths are full of lies,
whose right hand is sworn deceit.

[9] God, I will sing you a new song.
On the ten-stringed harp I will play for you—

[10] for the one who hands kings their victories,
who rescued David his servant from the cruel sword.

[11] Pull me out. Rescue me
from the grip of foreigners
whose mouths are full of lies,
whose right hand is sworn deceit.

[12] Then our sons will be like saplings
grown tall in their youth,
our daughters like pillars
carved for a palace.

[13] Our barns will overflow,
spilling every kind of provision.
Our flocks will multiply by thousands,
by tens of thousands in our fields.

[14] Our oxen will haul heavy loads.
No breach in the wall, no one carried off,
no scream rising in our streets.

[15] Blessed are the people who live like this.
Blessed are the people whose God is the LORD.

[1] I will lift you high, my God, my King. I will bless your name forever, and forever after that.

[2] Every day I will bless you. I will praise your name forever, and forever after that.

[3] The LORD is vast — beyond measure, beyond praise. His greatness has no floor, no ceiling, no edge to reach.

[4] One generation will hand your works to the next, will tell of your mighty acts.

[5] I will turn over in my mind the radiant splendor of your majesty, and the wonders you have done.

[6] They will speak of the terrifying force of your works, and I will recount your greatness.

[7] They will spill out the memory of your overflowing goodness and sing your justice with joy.

[8] The LORD is tender and full of mercy, slow to anger, abundant in steadfast love.

[9] The LORD is good to all. His compassion rests on everything he has made.

[10] All your works will give you thanks, LORD. Your faithful ones will bless you.

[11] They will speak of the glory of your kingdom and tell of your power,

[12] making your mighty acts known to the children of men, and the dazzling splendor of your reign.

[13] Your kingdom is a kingdom of all the ages. Your rule stretches across every generation that ever was or will be. The LORD is faithful in every word he speaks and gracious in everything he does.

[14] The LORD steadies all who are falling. He lifts every back that has been bent low.

[15] Every eye watches you in hope, and you give them their food in its proper time.

[16] You open your hand and satisfy the longing of every living thing.

[17] The LORD is just in all his ways and gracious in all his works.

[18] The LORD stands close to all who call on him — to all who call on him in truth.

[19] He fulfills the desire of those who fear him. He hears their cry and saves them.

[20] The LORD guards all who love him, but the wicked — every last one — he will destroy.

[21] My mouth will speak the praise of the LORD. Let every creature bless his holy name forever, and forever after that.

[1] Praise the LORD.

Praise the LORD, O my soul.

[2] I will praise the LORD as long as I draw breath. I will sing to my God with every heartbeat I have left.

[3] Don't stake your life on princes. Don't trust mortals to save you — they can't.

[4] One breath leaves them and they're back in the dirt. That very day, every plan they had dies with them.

[5] But blessed is the one whose help is the God of Jacob — whose hope rests on the LORD their God,

[6] the Maker of sky and earth and sea and everything in them, the One who keeps faith forever,

[7] who delivers justice to the crushed, who feeds the starving, who unlocks the prison door.

[8] The LORD gives sight to the blind. The LORD lifts up those bent double. The LORD loves the ones living right.

[9] The LORD watches over the foreigner, sustains the orphan and the widow — but he twists the path of the wicked into ruin.

[10] The LORD reigns forever. Your God, Zion, through every generation.

Praise the LORD.

[1] Praise the LORD. How good it is to sing to our God, how lovely, how fitting this song of praise.

[2] The LORD is rebuilding Jerusalem, gathering Israel's exiles home.

[3] He binds up the broken-hearted and dresses every wound.

[4] He counts the stars, every last one, and calls each by name.

[5] Our Lord is vast. His power, staggering. His understanding has no edge, no end.

[6] He lifts the humble off the ground and drives the wicked into the dirt.

[7] Sing to the LORD with thanksgiving. Strike up the harp for our God.

[8] He drapes the sky with clouds, sends rain to the earth, makes grass leap up on the hills.

[9] He feeds the cattle. He hears the raven chicks crying and answers.

[10] He doesn't thrill at the warhorse's strength or the runner's powerful legs.

[11] What pleases the LORD is this: those who revere him, those who stake everything on his unfailing love.

[12] Sing his praise, Jerusalem. Glorify your God, Zion.

[13] He has reinforced the bars of your gates and blessed the children inside your walls.

[14] He keeps your borders peaceful and fills you with the finest wheat.

[15] He sends his command to the earth — his word races out at full speed.

[16] He scatters snow like wool and spreads frost like ash.

[17] He hurls down hail like crumbs of bread — who can stand his cold?

[18] He speaks again, and it all melts. He stirs the wind, and waters flow.

[19] He gave his word to Jacob, his statutes and laws to Israel.

[20] He has done this for no other nation. They don't know his commands. Praise the LORD.

[1] Praise the LORD!
Praise him from the heavens —
praise him in the heights!

[2] Praise him, all his messengers.
Praise him, all his armies of light.

[3] Praise him, sun and moon.
Praise him, every shining star.

[4] Praise him, highest heavens,
and you waters above the sky.

[5] Let them all praise the name of the LORD —
he spoke, and they leapt into being.

[6] He set them in their places forever,
fixed them by a decree that will not break.

sea monsters and every ocean deep,

[8] fire and hail, snow and mist,

[9] mountains and every hill,
fruit trees and every cedar,

[10] wild beasts and all the cattle,

princes and every judge on the planet,

the song of every one of his faithful,

Praise the LORD!

[1] Sing to the LORD a song never sung before — let his praise rise in the gathering of the faithful. [2] Let Israel rejoice in the One who made them. [1] This is the unveiling of Jesus the Anointed, given to him by God to show his servants what is about to break loose. [3] Whoever reads these words out loud — blessed. Whoever hears them and holds onto what is written here — blessed. The time is right on top of us.

[4] John, to the seven assemblies in the province of Asia:

Grace to you and peace from the One who is, who was, and who is coming — and from the seven Spirits before his throne — [5] and from Jesus the Anointed, the faithful witness, the firstborn from among the dead, the ruler of every king on earth.

To the One who loves us and tore us free from our sins with his own blood, [6] who made us a kingdom, priests serving his God and Father — to him be the glory and the power forever and ever. Amen.

[9] I, John — your brother, sharing with you in the pressure and the kingdom and the patient endurance that come with Jesus — I was on the island called Patmos, exiled there for the word of God and the witness of Jesus. [10] On the Lord's day the Spirit took hold of me, and behind me I heard a voice like a war trumpet [11] saying: "Write down what you see in a scroll and send it to the seven assemblies — Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, Laodicea."

[12] I turned to see the voice that was speaking to me, and when I turned I saw seven golden lampstands, [13] and standing among the lampstands one like a son of man — robed to his feet, a gold sash across his chest. [14] His head and his hair white as wool, white as snow. His eyes — fire. [15] His feet like bronze glowing in a furnace. His voice like the roar of deep water. [16] In his right hand, seven stars. From his mouth, a sharp double-edged sword. His face — the sun blazing at full strength.

[17] When I saw him, I dropped at his feet like a corpse. He laid his right hand on me and said:

"Stop being afraid. I am the First and the Last. [18] I am the Living One. I was dead — and look, I am alive forever and ever. And I hold the keys to death and the grave.

[19] "So write down what you have seen, what is happening now, and what is about to happen after this. [20] Here is the secret of the seven stars you saw in my right hand, and of the seven golden lampstands: the seven stars are the messengers of the seven assemblies, and the seven lampstands are the assemblies themselves."

[1] To the messenger of the church in Ephesus, write this:

These are the words of the One who holds seven stars in his right hand, who walks among seven golden lampstands:

[2] I know what you've done. I know your labor, your endurance. I know you cannot stomach the corrupt — that you tested those who called themselves apostles and exposed them as frauds. [3] You've held the line. You've borne up under pressure for my name and refused to quit.

[4] But I have this against you: you've abandoned the love you had at the start. [5] Remember how high you were. Turn back. Do again what you did then. If you don't, I'm coming for you — and your lampstand comes off its stand.

[6] One thing in your favor: you hate what the Nicolaitans do. So do I.

[7] If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit is telling the churches: To the one who conquers, I will give the right to eat from the tree of life that grows in the paradise of God.

[8] To the messenger of the church in Smyrna, write this:

These are the words of the First and the Last, the One who died and came back:

[9] I know your suffering. I know your poverty — though you are rich. I know the slander leveled against you by those who call themselves Jews and are nothing of the kind. They are a synagogue of Satan.

[10] Don't be afraid of what's coming. The devil is about to throw some of you in prison to test you, and you'll suffer for ten days. Stay faithful — even if it kills you — and I will hand you the crown of life.

[11] If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit is telling the churches: The one who conquers walks away untouched by the second death.

[12] To the messenger of the church in Pergamum, write this:

These are the words of the One whose mouth wields a sharp two-edged sword:

[13] I know where you live — right where Satan's throne sits. And still you cling to my name. Even when Antipas, my faithful witness, was executed in your city — Satan's own backyard — you did not deny me.

[14] But I have a few things against you. Some of you cling to the teaching of Balaam, who showed Balak how to trip up Israel — luring them into idol-feasts and sexual sin. [15] You also have some who hold to the teaching of the Nicolaitans. [16] Turn back. Now. Or I'm coming, and I'll fight them with the sword from my mouth.

[17] If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit is telling the churches: To the one who conquers, I will give the hidden manna. I will give a white stone, and on it a new name — known to no one except the one who receives it.

[18] To the messenger of the church in Thyatira, write this:

These are the words of the Son of God, whose eyes blaze like fire, whose feet shine like burnished bronze:

[19] I know what you've done — your love, your faith, your service, your endurance. And your latest works exceed the first.

[20] But I have this against you: you tolerate that woman Jezebel — the one calling herself a prophet — who teaches my servants and seduces them into sexual sin and idol-feasts. [21] I gave her time to turn. She refuses. [22] So watch: I am throwing her onto a sickbed. And those who sleep with her — unless they repent of what she's done — I'll throw into deep suffering. [23] Her children I will strike dead. Then every church will know: I am the One who searches hearts and minds, and I will give back to each of you according to what you've done.

[24] But to the rest of you in Thyatira — those who haven't swallowed this teaching, who haven't dabbled in what they call "the deep things of Satan" — I lay no other burden on you. [25] Only hold on to what you have until I come.

[26] To the one who conquers and keeps doing my work to the end, I will give authority over the nations — [27] he will shepherd them with an iron rod, shattering them like clay pots — [28] the same authority I received from my Father. And I will give him the morning star.

[29] If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit is telling the churches.

[1] To the messenger of the assembly in Sardis, write:

These are the words of the One who holds the seven Spirits of God and the seven stars: I know what you've done. You have a reputation for being alive. You're dead.

[2] Wake up. Strengthen whatever's left before it dies too — because I have not found your works complete in the sight of my God.

[3] Remember what you received, what you heard. Hold onto it. Repent. If you won't wake up, I'll come like a thief, and you won't know the hour I arrive at your door.

[4] But a few names in Sardis haven't soiled their clothes. They will walk with me in white, because they are worthy.

[5] The one who conquers will be dressed in white. I will never erase that name from the book of life. I will speak that name out loud before my Father and his angels.

[6] If you have ears, hear what the Spirit is saying to the assemblies.

[7] To the messenger of the assembly in Philadelphia, write:

These are the words of the Holy One, the True One, the One who holds the key of David — what he opens no one shuts, what he shuts no one opens:
