[10] And on the house of David and the people of Jerusalem I will pour out a spirit of grace and desperate prayer. They will look at me — the one they ran through — and they will mourn him the way you mourn an only child, weeping bitterly the way you weep for a firstborn son.

[11] On that day the grief in Jerusalem will swell like the mourning at Hadad-Rimmon on the plain of Megiddo.

[12] The land will mourn, every family alone. The house of David alone, their women alone. The house of Nathan alone, their women alone.

[13] The house of Levi alone, their women alone. The house of Shimei alone, their women alone.

[14] Every remaining family — each one alone, and their women alone.

[1] On that day a fountain will break open for the house of David and for everyone who lives in Jerusalem — a fountain to wash away sin and the stain of it.

[2] On that day, declares the LORD of Armies, I will erase the names of the idols from the land — gone, forgotten. And the prophets too, and the unclean spirit driving them, I will sweep from the country.

[3] If anyone still tries to prophesy, his own father and mother — the ones who gave him life — will say to him: "You will not live. You have spoken lies in the name of the LORD." And as he prophesies, his own father and mother will run him through.

[4] On that day every prophet will be ashamed of his vision when he prophesies. He will no longer put on the rough cloak meant to deceive.

[5] He will insist, "I am no prophet. I work the soil. The land has been my living since I was young."

[6] And if someone asks, "Then what are these wounds on your chest?" — he will answer, "I got them in the house of those who loved me."

[7] Sword, wake up — go after my shepherd, the man who stands beside me, declares the LORD of Armies. Strike the shepherd, and watch the sheep scatter. And I will turn my hand against the little ones.

[8] Across the whole land, declares the LORD, two-thirds will be cut down and die. Only a third will be left.

[9] That third I will run through the fire. I will refine them the way silver is refined, test them the way gold is tested. They will call on my name, and I will answer. I will say, "They are mine." And they will say, "The LORD is our God."

[1] Listen — the day of the LORD is coming, and your plunder will be divided right inside your walls.

[2] I will gather every nation against Jerusalem for war. The city will fall. Houses ransacked. Women raped. Half the population marched into exile. But the rest of the people will not be cut off from the city.

[3] Then the LORD will march out and fight those nations the way he fights on the day of battle.

[4] His feet will touch down that day on the Mount of Olives, the ridge facing Jerusalem on the east — and the mountain will split in two, east to west, opening a vast valley. Half the mountain heaving north, half collapsing south.

[5] You will run through that mountain valley — the valley will stretch all the way to Azal — running like your ancestors ran from the earthquake in the days of Uzziah king of Judah. Then the LORD my God will arrive, every holy one with him.

[6] On that day there will be no light — no cold, no frost.

[7] One continuous day, known only to the LORD — neither day nor night — and when evening should fall, there will be light.

[8] On that day living water will pour out of Jerusalem, half toward the eastern sea, half toward the western — flowing summer and winter, never stopping.

[9] The LORD will be king over the whole earth. On that day, the LORD will be one and his name the only name.

[10] The whole land will flatten into a plain, from Geba to Rimmon south of Jerusalem. But Jerusalem itself will rise up and stay where she is — from the Benjamin Gate to the site of the First Gate, to the Corner Gate, and from the Tower of Hananel to the king's winepresses.

[11] People will live in her. Never again the curse. Jerusalem will dwell secure.

[12] And this is the plague the LORD will strike all the nations with — the ones who waged war against Jerusalem: their flesh will rot while they're still standing on their feet, their eyes will rot in their sockets, their tongues will rot in their mouths.

[13] On that day the LORD will send panic through them — terror like a contagion. Each man will grab his neighbor's hand and turn on him.

[14] Even Judah will fight at Jerusalem. The wealth of every surrounding nation will be gathered in — gold, silver, clothing — heaps of it.

[15] The same plague will fall on the horses, mules, camels, donkeys — every animal in those camps. Same wasting. Same horror.

[16] Then every survivor from all the nations that came against Jerusalem will make the pilgrimage year after year to worship the King, the LORD of Hosts, and to celebrate the Feast of Tabernacles.

[17] And any clan on earth that does not come up to Jerusalem to worship the King, the LORD of Hosts — no rain will fall on them.

[18] If Egypt's clan refuses to come up, refuses to appear — no rain for them either. They will get the plague the LORD strikes the nations with, the nations that won't come up to celebrate the Feast of Tabernacles.

[19] This will be Egypt's punishment. This will be the punishment of every nation that refuses to come up for the Feast of Tabernacles.

[20] On that day, the bells on the horses will be engraved: HOLY TO THE LORD. The cooking pots in the LORD's house will be as sacred as the bowls before the altar.

[21] Every pot in Jerusalem and Judah will be holy to the LORD of Hosts — anyone who comes to sacrifice can grab one and cook in it. And on that day, no more traders in the house of the LORD of Hosts. Not one.

[1] This is the word that came hammering down on Zephaniah son of Cushi, son of Gedaliah, son of Amariah, son of Hezekiah — in the days when Josiah son of Amon was king of Judah.

[2] I will sweep it all away. Everything. Off the face of the earth — God's declaration.

[3] I will sweep away human and animal. I will sweep away the birds of the sky and the fish of the sea — the wreckage piling up around the wicked. I will cut humanity off from the ground. God has spoken.

[4] I will stretch out my hand against Judah, against everyone who lives in Jerusalem. From this place I will erase every last trace of Baal, every name of every idol-priest —

[5] those who climb to their rooftops to bow to the starry host, those who bow to the Lord and swear by him while also swearing by Milcom,

[6] those who turned their backs and walked away from me, who never sought the Lord, never even bothered to ask.

[7] Silence! Hush before the Sovereign Lord — his Day is close. The Lord has prepared a sacrifice. He has consecrated his guests.

[8] On the Day of the Lord's sacrifice, I will come for the officials, for the king's sons, for everyone parading around in foreign clothes.

[9] On that day I will punish all who hop over the threshold — who fill their master's house with violence and lies.

[10] On that day — God's declaration — a cry will go up from the Fish Gate, a wail from the Second Quarter, a great crashing from the hills.

[11] Wail, you who live in the Mortar — every merchant is wiped out, every silver-counter cut down.

[12] At that hour I will hunt Jerusalem with lamps. I will go after the men sitting smug on the dregs of their wine, telling themselves, "The Lord won't act — neither for good nor for ill."

[13] Their wealth will be plundered. Their houses, demolished. They will build homes and never live in them. They will plant vineyards and never taste the wine.

[14] The great Day of the Lord is close — close and racing in fast. Listen — the Day of the Lord — even the warrior screams there in bitterness.

[15] That Day will be fury. A day of distress and anguish. A day of ruin and devastation. A day of darkness and gloom. A day of clouds and thick blackness.

[16] A day of the trumpet blast and the war-cry against the fortified cities, against the high corner towers.

[17] I will pin humanity down in terror. They will stumble around like the blind because they sinned against the Lord. Their blood will be poured out like dust, their guts like dung.

[18] Neither their silver nor their gold will save them on the Day of the Lord's fury. The whole earth will be devoured in the fire of his jealousy. He will make an end — a sudden, terrible end — of everyone who lives on the earth.

[1] Gather yourselves. Gather, you nation with no shame —

[2] before the decree drops, before the day blows past like chaff in the wind, before the heat of the LORD's anger crashes down on you, before the day of his fury arrives.

[3] Seek the LORD, all you humble of the land, you who actually do what he commands. Seek righteousness. Seek humility. Maybe — just maybe — you'll be hidden on the day the LORD's anger burns.

[4] Gaza will be abandoned. Ashkelon left in ruins. Ashdod driven out at noon. Ekron uprooted.

[5] Disaster on you who live along the sea, you Cherethite nation. The word of the LORD is against you, Canaan, land of the Philistines: I will destroy you. No one left.

[6] The coast where you live will become pastureland — shepherds' meadows, sheep pens.

[7] It will belong to the remnant of the house of Judah. There they'll graze their flocks. At evening they'll lie down in the houses of Ashkelon. The LORD their God will visit them and restore their fortunes.

[8] I've heard Moab's insults and the Ammonites' taunts — how they mocked my people and bragged against their borders.

[9] So as I live — declares the LORD of Hosts, the God of Israel — Moab will become another Sodom, the Ammonites another Gomorrah: a wasteland of weeds and salt pits, a desolation forever. What's left of my people will plunder them. The survivors of my nation will inherit their land.

[10] This is what their arrogance buys them — payment in full for insulting and strutting against the people of the LORD of Hosts.

[11] The LORD will terrify them. He will starve every god on earth into nothing. And the nations on every distant shore will bow down to him — each from their own place.

[12] You too, Cushites — run through by my sword.

[13] He will stretch out his hand against the north and shatter Assyria. He will turn Nineveh into a wasteland, dry as desert.

[14] Flocks will bed down in the middle of her — every kind of wild creature. The desert owl and the screech owl will roost on her columns. Their calls will echo through the windows. Rubble in the doorways. The cedar paneling stripped bare.

[15] This was the carefree city, sprawling in her own security, telling herself, *I am, and there is no one else.* Look at her now — a ruin, a den for animals. Everyone who passes by hisses and waves them off in disgust.

[1] Disaster waits for the rebel city — filthy, brutal, drunk on its own oppression.

[2] She refuses to listen. She won't accept correction. She doesn't trust the LORD. She won't come near her God.

[3] Her officials are lions mid-roar. Her judges are wolves at dusk — bones gnawed clean before morning.

[4] Her prophets are reckless, treacherous men. Her priests defile what is holy and twist the Law into something it was never meant to be.

[5] But the LORD is there in her streets — righteous, never wrong. Morning by morning he hands down his verdict, never failing, never absent. Yet the corrupt feel no shame.

[6] I have wiped out nations. I have leveled their fortresses. I emptied their streets — no one walking, no one left. Their cities lie in ruins. Not a soul, not a survivor.

[7] I thought, "Surely now she'll respect me. Surely now she'll accept correction." Then her dwelling wouldn't be erased, despite all the punishment I had prepared. But no — they rose up early and went right back to their corruption.

[8] So wait for me — this is the LORD speaking — wait for the day I rise to seize the prey. I have already decided: I will gather the nations, assemble the kingdoms, and pour my fury out on them — every burning ounce of my anger. The whole earth will be consumed in the fire of my jealousy.

[9] Then — then — I will give the peoples clean lips, so they can all call on the name of the LORD and serve him shoulder to shoulder.

[10] From beyond the rivers of Cush, my scattered worshipers, my exiled daughter, will bring me their offering.

[11] On that day, you won't have to carry the shame of all you did against me. I will remove from your midst the arrogant celebrators — and you will never again strut on my holy mountain.

[12] What I will leave with you is a people humble and lowly, who shelter in the name of the LORD.

[13] The remnant of Israel will do no wrong, speak no lies. No deceitful tongue will be found in their mouths. They will graze and lie down, and no one will make them afraid.

[14] Sing, daughter Zion! Shout it out, Israel! Let your whole heart break open with joy, daughter Jerusalem!

[15] The LORD has thrown out your sentence. He has turned back your enemy. The King of Israel — the LORD himself — is right here with you. You will never fear disaster again.

[16] On that day they will say to Jerusalem: Don't be afraid. Zion — let your hands hang strong, not slack.

[17] The LORD your God is in your midst — a warrior who saves. He will delight over you with joy. He will quiet you with his love. He will spin over you with singing.

[18] Those grieving the lost festivals — I will gather them in. They are yours. The shame they carried becomes a burden lifted.

[19] Watch me — at that time I will deal with everyone who crushed you. I will rescue the lame. I will gather the scattered. I will turn their shame into praise and rename their disgrace as renown in every land where they were humiliated.

[20] At that time I will bring you home — at that time I will gather you in. I will make your name known and your praise loud among all the peoples of the earth, when I restore your fortunes right before your eyes. The LORD has spoken.
